revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 田出井山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Tadeiyama Kofun zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
墳丘全景（右に前方部、左に後円部）
{{small|（堺市役所21F展望ロビーより）}} |所属 = [[百舌鳥古墳群]] |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[堺市]][[堺区]]北三国ヶ丘町2丁 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|34|32.65|N|135|29|18.31|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=田出井山古墳（伝反正天皇陵）}} |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長148[[メートル|m]]
高さ14.8m（前方部） |埋葬施設 = 不明 |築造年代 = [[5世紀]]中頃 |被葬者= （[[宮内庁]]治定）第18代[[反正天皇]] |出土品 = [[埴輪]]など |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「百舌鳥耳原北陵」 |特記事項 = |地図2={{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=田出井山
古墳|lat_deg=34|lat_min=34|lat_sec=32.65|lon_deg=135|lon_min=29|lon_sec=18.31|marker=place-of-worship|marksize=15|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=220|caption=大阪府内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|coord={{coord|34|34|32.65|N|135|29|18.31|E}}}}}} [[File:Tadeiyama Kofun haisho.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[反正天皇]]百舌鳥耳原北陵 拝所}}]] [[File:Tadeiyama Kofun Aerial photograph 2007.jpg|thumb|250px|田出井山古墳の空中写真（2007年）
{{国土航空写真}}]] '''田出井山古墳'''（たでいやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[堺区]]北三国ヶ丘町にある[[前方後円墳]]である。[[百舌鳥古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つで、実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''百舌鳥耳原北陵'''（もずのみみはらのきたのみささぎ）」として第18代[[反正天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されている。[[文化遺産 (世界遺産)|世界文化遺産]] [[百舌鳥・古市古墳群|百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-]] の構成資産の一部として登録されている。 == 概要 == [[百舌鳥古墳群]]の北端に位置し、大阪湾からの眺望を意識し、場所の選定と墳丘の方向が決めたと考えられ、台地の西縁部に等高線と墳丘主軸線を並行させるように前方部を南に向けて築造されている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000139|title=反正天皇陵古墳／構成資産／世界遺産／国指定文化財等データベース|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=文化庁}}。全長約148メートル、後円部径約76メートル、高さ約13メートル、前方部幅約110メートル、高さ約15メートル、墳丘は３段築成、くびれ部の西側のみに造り出しが作られ、盾形の内濠を有する{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/hanzeitenno.html|title=反正天皇陵古墳（田出井山古墳）　【世界文化遺産 構成資産】|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=堺市役所 文化観光局 博物館 学芸課}}。現在は一重の周濠だが、発掘調査により、築造時は外濠のある二重の周濠であったと判明し、また、円筒埴輪、形象埴輪、須恵器などが出土した。濠の形態や出土した[[埴輪]]より、5世紀中頃に造築されたと考えられている{{Sfn|百舌鳥古墳群（堺市）|2014年|p=13}}。周辺には[[陪塚]]と推定される鈴山古墳・天王古墳があり、それらも宮内庁が管理している{{Sfn|百舌鳥古墳群（堺市）|2014年|p=13}}。 江戸時代の絵図等では「反正天皇陵」「楯井陵」の表記が見られる。楯井は当地の広域地名でもある[[向井町|向井]]の別称で、田出井に転じたとされる。前方部側の北東角の内濠に[[方違神社]]境内が接するようにある。 天皇陵に比定されている百舌鳥耳原三陵のなかで、他の2つの古墳（[[大仙陵古墳]]、[[上石津ミサンザイ古墳]]）と比べて規模がかなり小さいことから、反正天皇陵であることを疑問視する意見も多く、宮内庁も[[土師ニサンザイ古墳]]（田出井山古墳よりも大きい）を反正天皇の[[陵墓参考地]]にしている。 1869年（明治2年）の測量である「反正帝御陵外廻り分見略図」が堺市立図書館に残る{{Cite web|和書|url=http://e-library.gprime.jp/lib_city_sakai/da/detail?tilcod=0000000013-S0011002 |title=反正帝御陵外廻り分見略図 |publisher =|accessdate =2018-07-14}}。 File:Suzuyama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|鈴山古墳 File:天王古墳.jpg|天王古墳 == 出土品 == 出土品として挙げられるものは、瑪瑙製勾玉、須恵器台がある。また、原位置を保っていないが円筒埴輪、形象埴輪（蓋型埴輪、家形埴輪、人物埴輪の手の部分）が出土している。出土した埴輪の特徴から五世紀後半でも古い段階のものであるとされている。反正天皇陵古墳の後円部北東側から円筒埴輪が多く出土している{{Cite book|和書|author= |title=堺市文化財調査概要報告 第1冊 |publisher=堺市教育委員会 |date=1990 |page= |isbn=}}。 == 規模 == 各古墳の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|百舌鳥古墳群（堺市）|2014年|p=13}}{{Cite book|和書|author=「ザ・古墳群」制作委員会 |title=ザ・古墳群〜百舌鳥と古市全89基 |publisher=中島淳 |year=2018 |page=103 |isbn=}}。 * 田出井山古墳 ** 墳丘長：148メートル ** 後円部 - 3段築成 *** 直径：76メートル *** 高さ：13メートル ** 前方部 - 3段築成 *** 幅：110メートル *** 高さ：14.8メートル * 鈴山古墳（倍塚）：形状は方墳{{Sfn|百舌鳥古墳群（堺市）|2014|p=13}}。 ** 墳丘長：22メートル ** 高さ：3メートル * 天王古墳（倍塚）：形状は方墳{{Sfn|百舌鳥古墳群（堺市）|2014|p=13}}。 ** 墳丘長：11メートル ** 高さ：3メートル == 周濠 == 墳丘は一重の周濠で囲まれているが、元は二重濠になっており、この二重濠については1980年（昭和55年）と1987年（昭和62年）に堺市教育委員会と宮内庁の調査によって確認されている。また、内濠と外濠との間の内堤が約23メートル、外濠幅が約11.5メートルであった{{Cite book|和書|author=水野正好 ほか|title=「天皇陵」総覧|year=|publisher=菅 英志|isbn=|page=150}}。外濠が埋まったのは、13世紀中ごろから14世紀後半と考えられる{{Sfn|百舌鳥古墳群（堺市）|2014|p=13}}。 == 調査概要 == 堺市で行われた発掘調査は、前方部東側（調査地1）、前方部南東隅（調査地2）、前方部南西隅西側（調査地3）、後円部北部（調査地4）で実施され、それぞれから外濠が検出された{{Sfn|古墳群の調査1|2008|pp=38–39}}。また、それら各調査地点以外でも、範囲確認調査、工事立ち合い調査により、外濠が検出された{{Sfn|古墳群の調査1|2008|pp=38–39}}。前方部側の外濠は調査数が少ないが、それ以外では、調査数が多く外濠の存在がほぼ確定してる{{Sfn|古墳群の調査1|2008|pp=38–39}}。 * 調査地1・前方部東側 - [[1980年]]（昭和55年）に、前方部墳丘南東隅から東側の濠対岸付近の私有地での発掘調査。外堀の外肩を検出し、外肩斜面は葺石が用いられ、深さは1メートル強であった。当調査地から道を挟んで内濠と外濠との間の内堤にあたる場所で、宮内庁による[[トレンチ調査 (考古学)|トレンチ調査]]が行われ、外濠の内肩が検出されたことから、外濠の幅が12メートルと判明した{{Sfn|古墳群の調査1|2008|pp=38–39}}。 * 調査地2・前方部南東隅 - [[1992年]]（平成4年）に前方部墳丘南東隅と濠の南東隅を結ぶ、延長線上の対岸付近の私有地での発掘調査。。外堀の南東の角の外肩を検出し、深さ0.7メートル以上、斜面角度25度を検出した{{Sfn|古墳群の調査1|2008|pp=38–39}}。 * 調査地3・前方部南西隅西側 - [[1987年]]（昭和62年）に前方部側の濠南東隅の西側付近の民間開発地での発掘調査。。外堀が検出され、幅は11.5メートル、深さ0.8メートル、斜面角度約22度で、外肩にのみ葺石が検出された{{Sfn|古墳群の調査1|2008|pp=38–39}}。 * 調査地4・後円部北部 - 2005年（平成17年）墳丘主軸線延長線上付近の民間開発地での発掘調査。。外濠の外肩が検出され、幅11 - 13メートル、深さ1メートル弱で、小振りな石の葺石が検出された{{Sfn|古墳群の調査1|2008|pp=38–39}}。 ::全ての発掘地点において、地山に削平が及んでいるため、外濠の幅、深さの値はさらに増すことが確実である。また、すべての地点で帯水していた状況を示す状況が検出されなかった。また地山に保水力が無いことから、外濠は、空堀であった可能性が高いと考えられた{{Sfn|古墳群の調査1|2008|pp=38–39}}。 範囲確認調査、工事立ち合いで、外濠が確認された地点は、古墳東側で3地点（うち一つをA地点とする）、古墳西側で2地点（D,E地点とする）である。 * 東側A地点 - 1984年（昭和59年）後円部東側の側方の内濠と外濠との間の内堤にあたる場所の私有地での範囲確認調査。外濠と想定される内肩が内濠側へ幅広く検出されたが、2次的な改変を伴っていたためと考えられた{{Sfn|古墳群の調査1|2008|pp=38–39}}。 * 西側D地点 - 2000年（平成12年）前方部東側の側方の外濠に相当する私有地での工事立ち合い調査。 外濠の外肩と想定される地山の傾斜を確認した{{Sfn|古墳群の調査1|2008|pp=38–39}}。 * 西側E地点 - 2002年（平成14年）後円部西側の側方の外濠に相当する民間開発地での工事立ち合い調査。2本のトレンチにより外濠幅が11.7メートルと判明し、深さは1.05メートルであったが、他の調査地点と異なる点は、主に粘土質が堆積していることから、帯水していた可能性が考えられた{{Sfn|古墳群の調査1|2008|pp=38–39}}。なお、開発地の道路設置が外濠を避ける位置に設計変更され、外濠が埋没保存された{{Sfn|古墳群の調査1|2008|pp=38–39}}。 ::各調査地点からは遺物が豊富に出土した。埴輪では、円筒埴輪、朝顔形埴輪、蓋型埴輪、人物埴輪などである。調査地2、3から5世紀代の須恵器台、調査地3からは、瑪瑙製の勾玉が出土した。円筒埴輪は、直径35センチメートル程度の中型品が主体だが、全容を残す個体が無いために、突帯の条数や器の高さは不明である{{Sfn|古墳群の調査1|2008|pp=38–39}}。 ::外濠は少なくとも、外肩に葺石を施し、内提、外提に埴輪を設置していたことが判明した。ただし、大部分の調査地で、帯水を示す状況がないことから、築造時の外濠は空堀であったと考えられる。外濠の平面の形状は、後円部よりも前方部がやや広がる盾形の形状で、規模は南北の墳丘主軸長で約270メートル、前方部での最大幅約250メートルと推測できた{{Sfn|古墳群の調査1|2008|pp=38–39}}。 ::内濠と外濠の後円部から前方部への開き具合や、円筒埴輪から考察すると、[[土師ニサンザイ古墳|ニサンザイ古墳]]よりも遡ることを示唆する結果となった{{Sfn|古墳群の調査1|2008|pp=38–39}}。 == 世界遺産 == [[2019年]]（令和元年）7月6日に世界文化遺産 '''百舌鳥・古市古墳群 -古代日本の墳墓群-''' を構成する百舌鳥古墳群の構成資産の一部として登録されている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=世界遺産／文化遺産オンライン|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=文化庁}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020|title=世界遺産／百舌鳥・古市古墳群―古代日本の墳墓群／国指定文化財等データベース|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=文化庁}}{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.mozu-furuichi.jp/jp/learn/mozu_furuichi_mozu_list.html|title=百舌鳥エリア古墳リスト／百舌鳥・古市古墳群|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=百舌鳥・古市古墳群世界遺産保存活用会議}}。 == 交通アクセス == * [[南海高野線]][[堺東駅]] 東へ徒歩5分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2014|chapter=|title=堺の文化財 百舌鳥古墳群|publisher=堺市文化観光局文化部文化財課|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|百舌鳥古墳群（堺市）|2014年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=百舌鳥古墳群の調査１|date=2008年3月31日|publisher=堺市教育委員会 発行|editor=堺市生涯学習部 文化財課 編集|ref={{SfnRef|古墳群の調査1|2008}}}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Tadeiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/018/index.html 百舌鳥耳原北陵] - 宮内庁 * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/hanzeitenno.html 反正天皇陵古墳（田出井山古墳）] - 堺市ホームページ *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/133 反正天皇陵古墳（田出井山古墳）] - 堺ナビ - （公社）堺観光コンベンション協会 - 本古墳の航空写真が掲載されている。 *[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/index.html 世界遺産「百舌鳥・古市古墳群」] - 堺市役所 {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たていやまこふん}} [[Category:百舌鳥古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:堺区の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]] [[Category:反正天皇]]

revision.comment: Created page with "[[ja:田出井山古墳]]"
revision.text: [[ja:田出井山古墳]]

[[ja:田出井山古墳]] [[File:Tadeiyama Kofun zenkei-2.jpg|280px|thumb|center|Full view of the tumulus (front mound on the right, rear mound on the left){{Small|(View from Sakai City Hall's 21F observation lobby)}}]] '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' (田出井山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺区}}, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The actual burial individual is unknown, but it is designated as "'''Mozu Mimihara Kitano Misasagi'''" (百舌鳥耳原北陵) by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, attributed to {{ill|Emperor Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}. It is also part of the {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}} site, "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."
== Overview == Tadeiyama Kofun is located at the northern edge of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The selection of its location and the orientation of its tumulus are thought to have been determined with consideration of the view of Osaka Bay. The tumulus was constructed along the western edge of a plateau, with the main axis of the mound parallel to the contour lines and the front mound facing south.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000139|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus / Components / World Heritage / National Cultural Properties Database|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
The tumulus has a total length of approximately 148 meters. The rear mound has a diameter of about 76 meters and a height of about 13 meters, while the front mound has a width of about 110 meters and a height of approximately 15 meters. The mound is built in three tiers and features a protrusion only on the western side of the constriction area. It also has a shield-shaped inner moat.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/hanzeitenno.html|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun) - World Heritage Site Component Asset|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Sakai City Cultural and Tourism Bureau, Museum and Archaeology Division}}</ref>
Excavations revealed that the mound originally had a double moat structure. Cylindrical haniwa, haniwa in various shapes, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been unearthed from the site. Based on these artifacts, it is estimated that the tumulus was constructed in the mid-5th century.<ref name=":0" /> The Suzuyama Kofun and Tenno Kofun, thought to be associated satellite tumuli (陪塚), are also managed by the Imperial Household Agency.<ref name=":0" />
Historical maps and documents from the Edo period describe the site as "Hanzei Tennō-ryō" or "Tatei-no-misasagi." "Tatei" was a regional name that later evolved into "Tadei." The inner moat at the northeastern corner of the front mound is adjacent to the grounds of the nearby {{ill|Katanokami Shrine|ja|方違神社}}.
There is debate about the attribution of this tumulus as Emperor Hanzei's mausoleum due to its significantly smaller size compared to the other two tumuli identified as part of the Mozu Mimihara Sanryō (三陵): {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}} and {{ill|Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has also designated the larger {{ill|Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳}} as an auxiliary burial site for Emperor Hanzei.
<gallery> File:Suzuyama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|Suzuyama Kofun File:天王古墳.jpg|Tenno Kofun </gallery>
== Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts unearthed from the site include magatama made of agate, Sue ware vessels, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa, lid-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and parts of human-shaped haniwa. The haniwa fragments suggest a relatively early phase of the 5th century. Numerous cylindrical haniwa fragments have been found on the northeastern side of the rear mound.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sakai Cultural Heritage Research Overview, Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|year=1990}}</ref>
== Scale == The dimensions of the tumuli are as follows:<ref>{{Cite book|author="The Kofun Group" Production Committee|title=The Kofun Group: Mozu and Furuichi - All 89 Mounds|publisher=Nakashima Atsushi|year=2018|page=103}}</ref> * **Tadeiyama Kofun** * Total length: 148 meters * **Rear mound** (three tiers): * Diameter: 76 meters * Height: 13 meters * **Front mound** (three tiers): * Width: 110 meters * Height: 14.8 meters * **Suzuyama Kofun (satellite mound)**: Square-shaped tumulus * Total length: 22 meters * Height: 3 meters * **Tenno Kofun (satellite mound)**: Square-shaped tumulus * Total length: 11 meters * Height: 3 meters
== World Heritage == In July 2019, Tadeiyama Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Heritage / Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020|title=World Heritage / Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
== Access == * **Train**: A 5-minute walk east from {{ill|Sakai Higashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the Nankai Koya Line.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tadeiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/018/index.html Mozu Mimihara Kitano Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/hanzeitenno.html Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun)] - Sakai City Website * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/133 Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun)] - Sakai Tourism and Convention Bureau * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/index.html World Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group] - Sakai City Office

The haniwa fragments suggest a relatively early phase of the 5th century. Numerous cylindrical haniwa fragments have been found on the northeastern side of the rear mound.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sakai Cultural Heritage Research Overview, Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|year=1990}}</ref>
== Scale == The dimensions of the tumuli are as follows:<ref>{{Cite book|author="The Kofun Group" Production Committee|title=The Kofun Group: Mozu and Furuichi - All 89 Mounds|publisher=Nakashima Atsushi|year=2018|page=103}}</ref> * **Tadeiyama Kofun** * Total length: 148 meters * **Rear mound** (three tiers): * Diameter: 76 meters * Height: 13 meters * **Front mound** (three tiers): * Width: 110 meters * Height: 14.8 meters * **Suzuyama Kofun (satellite mound)**: Square-shaped tumulus * Total length: 22 meters * Height: 3 meters * **Tenno Kofun (satellite mound)**: Square-shaped tumulus * Total length: 11 meters * Height: 3 meters
== World Heritage == In July 2019, Tadeiyama Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Heritage / Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020|title=World Heritage / Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
== Access == * **Train**: A 5-minute walk east from {{ill|Sakai Higashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the Nankai Koya Line.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tadeiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/018/index.html Mozu Mimihara Kitano Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/hanzeitenno.html Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun)] - Sakai City Website * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/133 Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun)] - Sakai Tourism and Convention Bureau * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/index.html World Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group] - Sakai City Office
[[Category:Mozu Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture's historical sites]] [[Category:Sakai-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Hanzei]] [[category:new pages]][[category:new pages]][[ja:田出井山古墳]]

[[category:new pages]][[category:new pages]][[ja:田出井山古墳]] [[File:Tadeiyama Kofun zenkei-2.jpg|280px|thumb|center|Full view of the tumulus (front mound on the right, rear mound on the left){{Small|(View from Sakai City Hall's 21F observation lobby)}}]] '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' (田出井山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺区}}, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The actual burial individual is unknown, but it is designated as "'''Mozu Mimihara Kitano Misasagi'''" (百舌鳥耳原北陵) by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, attributed to {{ill|Emperor Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}. It is also part of the {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}} site, "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."
== Overview == Tadeiyama Kofun is located at the northern edge of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The selection of its location and the orientation of its tumulus are thought to have been determined with consideration of the view of Osaka Bay. The tumulus was constructed along the western edge of a plateau, with the main axis of the mound parallel to the contour lines and the front mound facing south.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000139|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus / Components / World Heritage / National Cultural Properties Database|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
The tumulus has a total length of approximately 148 meters. The rear mound has a diameter of about 76 meters and a height of about 13 meters, while the front mound has a width of about 110 meters and a height of approximately 15 meters. The mound is built in three tiers and features a protrusion only on the western side of the constriction area. It also has a shield-shaped inner moat.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/hanzeitenno.html|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun) - World Heritage Site Component Asset|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Sakai City Cultural and Tourism Bureau, Museum and Archaeology Division}}</ref>
Excavations revealed that the mound originally had a double moat structure. Cylindrical haniwa, haniwa in various shapes, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been unearthed from the site. Based on these artifacts, it is estimated that the tumulus was constructed in the mid-5th century.<ref name=":0" /> The Suzuyama Kofun and Tenno Kofun, thought to be associated satellite tumuli (陪塚), are also managed by the Imperial Household Agency.<ref name=":0" />
Historical maps and documents from the Edo period describe the site as "Hanzei Tennō-ryō" or "Tatei-no-misasagi." "Tatei" was a regional name that later evolved into "Tadei." The inner moat at the northeastern corner of the front mound is adjacent to the grounds of the nearby {{ill|Katanokami Shrine|ja|方違神社}}.
There is debate about the attribution of this tumulus as Emperor Hanzei's mausoleum due to its significantly smaller size compared to the other two tumuli identified as part of the Mozu Mimihara Sanryō (三陵): {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}} and {{ill|Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has also designated the larger {{ill|Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳}} as an auxiliary burial site for Emperor Hanzei.
<gallery> File:Suzuyama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|Suzuyama Kofun File:天王古墳.jpg|Tenno Kofun </gallery>
== Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts unearthed from the site include magatama made of agate, Sue ware vessels, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa, lid-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and parts of human-shaped haniwa. The haniwa fragments suggest a relatively early phase of the 5th century. Numerous cylindrical haniwa fragments have been found on the northeastern side of the rear mound.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sakai Cultural Heritage Research Overview, Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|year=1990}}</ref>
== Scale == The dimensions of the tumuli are as follows:<ref>{{Cite book|author="The Kofun Group" Production Committee|title=The Kofun Group: Mozu and Furuichi - All 89 Mounds|publisher=Nakashima Atsushi|year=2018|page=103}}</ref> * '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' * Total length: 148 meters * '''Rear mound''' (three tiers): * Diameter: 76 meters * Height: 13 meters * '''Front mound''' (three tiers): * Width: 110 meters * Height: 14.8 meters * '''Suzuyama Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus * Total length: 22 meters * Height: 3 meters * '''Tenno Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus * Total length: 11 meters * Height: 3 meters
== World Heritage == In July 2019, Tadeiyama Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Heritage / Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020|title=World Heritage / Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
== Access == * '''Train''': A 5-minute walk east from {{ill|Sakai Higashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the Nankai Koya Line.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tadeiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/018/index.html Mozu Mimihara Kitano Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/hanzeitenno.html Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun)] - Sakai City Website * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/133 Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun)] - Sakai Tourism and Convention Bureau * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/index.html World Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group] - Sakai City Office

Based on these artifacts, it is estimated that the tumulus was constructed in the mid-5th century.<ref name=":0" /> The Suzuyama Kofun and Tenno Kofun, thought to be associated satellite tumuli (陪塚), are also managed by the Imperial Household Agency.<ref name=":0" />
Historical maps and documents from the Edo period describe the site as "Hanzei Tennō-ryō" or "Tatei-no-misasagi." "Tatei" was a regional name that later evolved into "Tadei." The inner moat at the northeastern corner of the front mound is adjacent to the grounds of the nearby {{ill|Katanokami Shrine|ja|方違神社}}.
There is debate about the attribution of this tumulus as Emperor Hanzei's mausoleum due to its significantly smaller size compared to the other two tumuli identified as part of the Mozu Mimihara Sanryō (三陵): {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}} and {{ill|Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has also designated the larger {{ill|Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳}} as an auxiliary burial site for Emperor Hanzei.
<gallery> File:Suzuyama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|Suzuyama Kofun File:天王古墳.jpg|Tenno Kofun </gallery>
== Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts unearthed from the site include magatama made of agate, Sue ware vessels, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa, lid-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and parts of human-shaped haniwa. The haniwa fragments suggest a relatively early phase of the 5th century. Numerous cylindrical haniwa fragments have been found on the northeastern side of the rear mound.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sakai Cultural Heritage Research Overview, Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|year=1990}}</ref>
== Scale == The dimensions of the tumuli are as follows:<ref>{{Cite book|author="The Kofun Group" Production Committee|title=The Kofun Group: Mozu and Furuichi - All 89 Mounds|publisher=Nakashima Atsushi|year=2018|page=103}}</ref> * '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' * Total length: 148 meters * '''Rear mound''' (three tiers): * Diameter: 76 meters * Height: 13 meters * '''Front mound''' (three tiers): * Width: 110 meters * Height: 14.8 meters * '''Suzuyama Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus * Total length: 22 meters * Height: 3 meters * '''Tenno Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus * Total length: 11 meters * Height: 3 meters
== World Heritage == In July 2019, Tadeiyama Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Heritage / Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020|title=World Heritage / Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
== Access == * '''Train''': A 5-minute walk east from {{ill|Sakai Higashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the Nankai Koya Line.

[[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tadeiyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tadeiyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[ja:田出井山古墳]] [[File:Tadeiyama Kofun zenkei-2.jpg|280px|thumb|center|Full view of the tumulus (front mound on the right, rear mound on the left){{Small|(View from Sakai City Hall's 21F observation lobby)}}]] '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' (田出井山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺区}}, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The actual burial individual is unknown, but it is designated as "'''Mozu Mimihara Kitano Misasagi'''" (百舌鳥耳原北陵) by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, attributed to {{ill|Emperor Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}. It is also part of the {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}} site, "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."
== Overview == Tadeiyama Kofun is located at the northern edge of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The selection of its location and the orientation of its tumulus are thought to have been determined with consideration of the view of Osaka Bay. The tumulus was constructed along the western edge of a plateau, with the main axis of the mound parallel to the contour lines and the front mound facing south.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000139|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus / Components / World Heritage / National Cultural Properties Database|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
The tumulus has a total length of approximately 148 meters. The rear mound has a diameter of about 76 meters and a height of about 13 meters, while the front mound has a width of about 110 meters and a height of approximately 15 meters. The mound is built in three tiers and features a protrusion only on the western side of the constriction area. It also has a shield-shaped inner moat.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/hanzeitenno.html|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun) - World Heritage Site Component Asset|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Sakai City Cultural and Tourism Bureau, Museum and Archaeology Division}}</ref>
Excavations revealed that the mound originally had a double moat structure. Cylindrical haniwa, haniwa in various shapes, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been unearthed from the site. Based on these artifacts, it is estimated that the tumulus was constructed in the mid-5th century.<ref name=":0" /> The Suzuyama Kofun and Tenno Kofun, thought to be associated satellite tumuli (陪塚), are also managed by the Imperial Household Agency.<ref name=":0" />
Historical maps and documents from the Edo period describe the site as "Hanzei Tennō-ryō" or "Tatei-no-misasagi." "Tatei" was a regional name that later evolved into "Tadei." The inner moat at the northeastern corner of the front mound is adjacent to the grounds of the nearby {{ill|Katanokami Shrine|ja|方違神社}}.
There is debate about the attribution of this tumulus as Emperor Hanzei's mausoleum due to its significantly smaller size compared to the other two tumuli identified as part of the Mozu Mimihara Sanryō (三陵): {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}} and {{ill|Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has also designated the larger {{ill|Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳}} as an auxiliary burial site for Emperor Hanzei.
<gallery> File:Suzuyama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|Suzuyama Kofun File:天王古墳.jpg|Tenno Kofun </gallery>
== Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts unearthed from the site include magatama made of agate, Sue ware vessels, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa, lid-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and parts of human-shaped haniwa. The haniwa fragments suggest a relatively early phase of the 5th century. Numerous cylindrical haniwa fragments have been found on the northeastern side of the rear mound.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sakai Cultural Heritage Research Overview, Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|year=1990}}</ref>
== Scale == The dimensions of the tumuli are as follows:<ref>{{Cite book|author="The Kofun Group" Production Committee|title=The Kofun Group: Mozu and Furuichi - All 89 Mounds|publisher=Nakashima Atsushi|year=2018|page=103}}</ref> * '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' * Total length: 148 meters * '''Rear mound''' (three tiers): * Diameter: 76 meters * Height: 13 meters * '''Front mound''' (three tiers): * Width: 110 meters * Height: 14.8 meters * '''Suzuyama Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus * Total length: 22 meters * Height: 3 meters * '''Tenno Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus * Total length: 11 meters * Height: 3 meters
== World Heritage == In July 2019, Tadeiyama Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Heritage / Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020|title=World Heritage / Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>

revision.comment: Fixing chatGPT error in bullet points
revision.text:
[[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tadeiyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tadeiyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[ja:田出井山古墳]] [[File:Tadeiyama Kofun zenkei-2.jpg|280px|thumb|center|Full view of the tumulus (front mound on the right, rear mound on the left){{Small|(View from Sakai City Hall's 21F observation lobby)}}]] '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' (田出井山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺区}}, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The actual burial individual is unknown, but it is designated as "'''Mozu Mimihara Kitano Misasagi'''" (百舌鳥耳原北陵) by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, attributed to {{ill|Emperor Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}. It is also part of the {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}} site, "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."
== Overview == Tadeiyama Kofun is located at the northern edge of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The selection of its location and the orientation of its tumulus are thought to have been determined with consideration of the view of Osaka Bay. The tumulus was constructed along the western edge of a plateau, with the main axis of the mound parallel to the contour lines and the front mound facing south.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000139|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus / Components / World Heritage / National Cultural Properties Database|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
The tumulus has a total length of approximately 148 meters. The rear mound has a diameter of about 76 meters and a height of about 13 meters, while the front mound has a width of about 110 meters and a height of approximately 15 meters. The mound is built in three tiers and features a protrusion only on the western side of the constriction area. It also has a shield-shaped inner moat.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/hanzeitenno.html|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun) - World Heritage Site Component Asset|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Sakai City Cultural and Tourism Bureau, Museum and Archaeology Division}}</ref>
Excavations revealed that the mound originally had a double moat structure. Cylindrical haniwa, haniwa in various shapes, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been unearthed from the site. Based on these artifacts, it is estimated that the tumulus was constructed in the mid-5th century.<ref name=":0" /> The Suzuyama Kofun and Tenno Kofun, thought to be associated satellite tumuli (陪塚), are also managed by the Imperial Household Agency.<ref name=":0" />
Historical maps and documents from the Edo period describe the site as "Hanzei Tennō-ryō" or "Tatei-no-misasagi." "Tatei" was a regional name that later evolved into "Tadei." The inner moat at the northeastern corner of the front mound is adjacent to the grounds of the nearby {{ill|Katanokami Shrine|ja|方違神社}}.
There is debate about the attribution of this tumulus as Emperor Hanzei's mausoleum due to its significantly smaller size compared to the other two tumuli identified as part of the Mozu Mimihara Sanryō (三陵): {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}} and {{ill|Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has also designated the larger {{ill|Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳}} as an auxiliary burial site for Emperor Hanzei.
<gallery> File:Suzuyama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|Suzuyama Kofun File:天王古墳.jpg|Tenno Kofun </gallery>
== Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts unearthed from the site include magatama made of agate, Sue ware vessels, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa, lid-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and parts of human-shaped haniwa. The haniwa fragments suggest a relatively early phase of the 5th century. Numerous cylindrical haniwa fragments have been found on the northeastern side of the rear mound.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sakai Cultural Heritage Research Overview, Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|year=1990}}</ref>
== Scale == The dimensions of the tumuli are as follows:<ref>{{Cite book|author="The Kofun Group" Production Committee|title=The Kofun Group: Mozu and Furuichi - All 89 Mounds|publisher=Nakashima Atsushi|year=2018|page=103}}</ref> * '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' ** Total length: 148 meters ** '''Rear mound''' (three tiers): ** Diameter: 76 meters ** Height: 13 meters ** '''Front mound''' (three tiers): ** Width: 110 meters ** Height: 14.8 meters * '''Suzuyama Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 22 meters ** Height: 3 meters * '''Tenno Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 11 meters ** Height: 3 meters
== World Heritage == In July 2019, Tadeiyama Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Heritage / Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020|title=World Heritage / Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
== Access == * '''Train''': A 5-minute walk east from {{ill|Sakai Higashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the Nankai Koya Line.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tadeiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/018/index.html Mozu Mimihara Kitano Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/hanzeitenno.html Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun)] - Sakai City Website * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/133 Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun)] - Sakai Tourism and Convention Bureau * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/index.html World Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group] - Sakai City Office

Historical maps and documents from the Edo period describe the site as "Hanzei Tennō-ryō" or "Tatei-no-misasagi." "Tatei" was a regional name that later evolved into "Tadei." The inner moat at the northeastern corner of the front mound is adjacent to the grounds of the nearby {{ill|Katanokami Shrine|ja|方違神社}}.
There is debate about the attribution of this tumulus as Emperor Hanzei's mausoleum due to its significantly smaller size compared to the other two tumuli identified as part of the Mozu Mimihara Sanryō (三陵): {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}} and {{ill|Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has also designated the larger {{ill|Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳}} as an auxiliary burial site for Emperor Hanzei.
<gallery> File:Suzuyama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|Suzuyama Kofun File:天王古墳.jpg|Tenno Kofun </gallery>
== Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts unearthed from the site include magatama made of agate, Sue ware vessels, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa, lid-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and parts of human-shaped haniwa. The haniwa fragments suggest a relatively early phase of the 5th century. Numerous cylindrical haniwa fragments have been found on the northeastern side of the rear mound.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sakai Cultural Heritage Research Overview, Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|year=1990}}</ref>
== Scale == The dimensions of the tumuli are as follows:<ref>{{Cite book|author="The Kofun Group" Production Committee|title=The Kofun Group: Mozu and Furuichi - All 89 Mounds|publisher=Nakashima Atsushi|year=2018|page=103}}</ref> * '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' ** Total length: 148 meters ** '''Rear mound''' (three tiers): ** Diameter: 76 meters ** Height: 13 meters ** '''Front mound''' (three tiers): ** Width: 110 meters ** Height: 14.8 meters * '''Suzuyama Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 22 meters ** Height: 3 meters * '''Tenno Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 11 meters ** Height: 3 meters
== World Heritage == In July 2019, Tadeiyama Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Heritage / Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020|title=World Heritage / Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
== Access == * '''Train''': A 5-minute walk east from {{ill|Sakai Higashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the Nankai Koya Line.

[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tadeiyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tadeiyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[ja:田出井山古墳]] [[File:Tadeiyama Kofun zenkei-2.jpg|280px|thumb|center|Full view of the tumulus (front mound on the right, rear mound on the left){{Small|(View from Sakai City Hall's 21F observation lobby)}}]] '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' (田出井山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺区}}, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The actual burial individual is unknown, but it is designated as "'''Mozu Mimihara Kitano Misasagi'''" (百舌鳥耳原北陵) by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, attributed to {{ill|Emperor Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}. It is also part of the {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}} site, "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."
== Overview == Tadeiyama Kofun is located at the northern edge of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The selection of its location and the orientation of its tumulus are thought to have been determined with consideration of the view of Osaka Bay. The tumulus was constructed along the western edge of a plateau, with the main axis of the mound parallel to the contour lines and the front mound facing south.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000139|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus / Components / World Heritage / National Cultural Properties Database|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
The tumulus has a total length of approximately 148 meters. The rear mound has a diameter of about 76 meters and a height of about 13 meters, while the front mound has a width of about 110 meters and a height of approximately 15 meters. The mound is built in three tiers and features a protrusion only on the western side of the constriction area. It also has a shield-shaped inner moat.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/hanzeitenno.html|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun) - World Heritage Site Component Asset|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Sakai City Cultural and Tourism Bureau, Museum and Archaeology Division}}</ref>
Excavations revealed that the mound originally had a double moat structure. Cylindrical haniwa, haniwa in various shapes, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been unearthed from the site. Based on these artifacts, it is estimated that the tumulus was constructed in the mid-5th century.<ref name=":0" /> The Suzuyama Kofun and Tenno Kofun, thought to be associated satellite tumuli (陪塚), are also managed by the Imperial Household Agency.<ref name=":0" />
Historical maps and documents from the Edo period describe the site as "Hanzei Tennō-ryō" or "Tatei-no-misasagi." "Tatei" was a regional name that later evolved into "Tadei." The inner moat at the northeastern corner of the front mound is adjacent to the grounds of the nearby {{ill|Katanokami Shrine|ja|方違神社}}.
There is debate about the attribution of this tumulus as Emperor Hanzei's mausoleum due to its significantly smaller size compared to the other two tumuli identified as part of the Mozu Mimihara Sanryō (三陵): {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}} and {{ill|Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has also designated the larger {{ill|Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳}} as an auxiliary burial site for Emperor Hanzei.
<gallery> File:Suzuyama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|Suzuyama Kofun File:天王古墳.jpg|Tenno Kofun </gallery>
== Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts unearthed from the site include magatama made of agate, Sue ware vessels, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa, lid-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and parts of human-shaped haniwa. The haniwa fragments suggest a relatively early phase of the 5th century. Numerous cylindrical haniwa fragments have been found on the northeastern side of the rear mound.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sakai Cultural Heritage Research Overview, Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|year=1990}}</ref>
== Scale == The dimensions of the tumuli are as follows:<ref>{{Cite book|author="The Kofun Group" Production Committee|title=The Kofun Group: Mozu and Furuichi - All 89 Mounds|publisher=Nakashima Atsushi|year=2018|page=103}}</ref> * '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' ** Total length: 148 meters ** '''Rear mound''' (three tiers): ** Diameter: 76 meters ** Height: 13 meters ** '''Front mound''' (three tiers): ** Width: 110 meters ** Height: 14.8 meters * '''Suzuyama Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 22 meters ** Height: 3 meters * '''Tenno Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 11 meters ** Height: 3 meters
== World Heritage == In July 2019, Tadeiyama Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Heritage / Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020|title=World Heritage / Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
== Access == * '''Train''': A 5-minute walk east from {{ill|Sakai Higashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the Nankai Koya Line.

[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tadeiyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tadeiyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[ja:田出井山古墳]] [[File:Tadeiyama Kofun zenkei-2.jpg|280px|thumb|center|Full view of the tumulus (front mound on the right, rear mound on the left){{Small|(View from Sakai City Hall's 21F observation lobby)}}]] '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' (田出井山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺区}}, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The actual burial individual is unknown, but it is designated as "'''Mozu Mimihara Kitano Misasagi'''" (百舌鳥耳原北陵) by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, attributed to {{ill|Emperor Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}. It is also part of the {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}} site, "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."
== Overview == Tadeiyama Kofun is located at the northern edge of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The selection of its location and the orientation of its tumulus are thought to have been determined with consideration of the view of Osaka Bay. The tumulus was constructed along the western edge of a plateau, with the main axis of the mound parallel to the contour lines and the front mound facing south.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000139|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus / Components / World Heritage / National Cultural Properties Database|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
The tumulus has a total length of approximately 148 meters. The rear mound has a diameter of about 76 meters and a height of about 13 meters, while the front mound has a width of about 110 meters and a height of approximately 15 meters. The mound is built in three tiers and features a protrusion only on the western side of the constriction area. It also has a shield-shaped inner moat.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/hanzeitenno.html|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun) - World Heritage Site Component Asset|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Sakai City Cultural and Tourism Bureau, Museum and Archaeology Division}}</ref>
Excavations revealed that the mound originally had a double moat structure. Cylindrical haniwa, haniwa in various shapes, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been unearthed from the site. Based on these artifacts, it is estimated that the tumulus was constructed in the mid-5th century.<ref name=":0" /> The Suzuyama Kofun and Tenno Kofun, thought to be associated satellite tumuli (陪塚), are also managed by the Imperial Household Agency.<ref name=":0" />
Historical maps and documents from the Edo period describe the site as "Hanzei Tennō-ryō" or "Tatei-no-misasagi." "Tatei" was a regional name that later evolved into "Tadei." The inner moat at the northeastern corner of the front mound is adjacent to the grounds of the nearby {{ill|Katanokami Shrine|ja|方違神社}}.
There is debate about the attribution of this tumulus as Emperor Hanzei's mausoleum due to its significantly smaller size compared to the other two tumuli identified as part of the Mozu Mimihara Sanryō (三陵): {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}} and {{ill|Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has also designated the larger {{ill|Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳}} as an auxiliary burial site for Emperor Hanzei.
<gallery> File:Suzuyama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|Suzuyama Kofun File:天王古墳.jpg|Tenno Kofun </gallery>
== Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts unearthed from the site include magatama made of agate, Sue ware vessels, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa, lid-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and parts of human-shaped haniwa. The haniwa fragments suggest a relatively early phase of the 5th century. Numerous cylindrical haniwa fragments have been found on the northeastern side of the rear mound.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sakai Cultural Heritage Research Overview, Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|year=1990}}</ref>
== Scale == The dimensions of the tumuli are as follows:<ref>{{Cite book|author="The Kofun Group" Production Committee|title=The Kofun Group: Mozu and Furuichi - All 89 Mounds|publisher=Nakashima Atsushi|year=2018|page=103}}</ref> * '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' ** Total length: 148 meters ** '''Rear mound''' (three tiers): ** Diameter: 76 meters ** Height: 13 meters ** '''Front mound''' (three tiers): ** Width: 110 meters ** Height: 14.8 meters * '''Suzuyama Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 22 meters ** Height: 3 meters * '''Tenno Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 11 meters ** Height: 3 meters
== World Heritage == In July 2019, Tadeiyama Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Heritage / Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020|title=World Heritage / Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
== Access == * '''Train''': A 5-minute walk east from {{ill|Sakai Higashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the Nankai Koya Line.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tadeiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/018/index.html Mozu Mimihara Kitano Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/hanzeitenno.html Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun)] - Sakai City Website * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/133 Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun)] - Sakai Tourism and Convention Bureau * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/index.html World Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group] - Sakai City Office

There is debate about the attribution of this tumulus as Emperor Hanzei's mausoleum due to its significantly smaller size compared to the other two tumuli identified as part of the Mozu Mimihara Sanryō (三陵): {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}} and {{ill|Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has also designated the larger {{ill|Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳}} as an auxiliary burial site for Emperor Hanzei.
<gallery> File:Suzuyama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|Suzuyama Kofun File:天王古墳.jpg|Tenno Kofun </gallery>
== Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts unearthed from the site include magatama made of agate, Sue ware vessels, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa, lid-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and parts of human-shaped haniwa. The haniwa fragments suggest a relatively early phase of the 5th century. Numerous cylindrical haniwa fragments have been found on the northeastern side of the rear mound.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sakai Cultural Heritage Research Overview, Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|year=1990}}</ref>
== Scale == The dimensions of the tumuli are as follows:<ref>{{Cite book|author="The Kofun Group" Production Committee|title=The Kofun Group: Mozu and Furuichi - All 89 Mounds|publisher=Nakashima Atsushi|year=2018|page=103}}</ref> * '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' ** Total length: 148 meters ** '''Rear mound''' (three tiers): ** Diameter: 76 meters ** Height: 13 meters ** '''Front mound''' (three tiers): ** Width: 110 meters ** Height: 14.8 meters * '''Suzuyama Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 22 meters ** Height: 3 meters * '''Tenno Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 11 meters ** Height: 3 meters
== World Heritage == In July 2019, Tadeiyama Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Heritage / Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020|title=World Heritage / Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
== Access == * '''Train''': A 5-minute walk east from {{ill|Sakai Higashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the Nankai Koya Line.

It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The actual burial individual is unknown, but it is designated as "'''Mozu Mimihara Kitano Misasagi'''" (百舌鳥耳原北陵) by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, attributed to {{ill|Emperor Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}. It is also part of the {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}} site, "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."
== Overview == Tadeiyama Kofun is located at the northern edge of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The selection of its location and the orientation of its tumulus are thought to have been determined with consideration of the view of Osaka Bay. The tumulus was constructed along the western edge of a plateau, with the main axis of the mound parallel to the contour lines and the front mound facing south.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000139|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus / Components / World Heritage / National Cultural Properties Database|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
The tumulus has a total length of approximately 148 meters. The rear mound has a diameter of about 76 meters and a height of about 13 meters, while the front mound has a width of about 110 meters and a height of approximately 15 meters. The mound is built in three tiers and features a protrusion only on the western side of the constriction area. It also has a shield-shaped inner moat.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/hanzeitenno.html|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun) - World Heritage Site Component Asset|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Sakai City Cultural and Tourism Bureau, Museum and Archaeology Division}}</ref>
Excavations revealed that the mound originally had a double moat structure. Cylindrical haniwa, haniwa in various shapes, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been unearthed from the site. Based on these artifacts, it is estimated that the tumulus was constructed in the mid-5th century.<ref name=":0" /> The Suzuyama Kofun and Tenno Kofun, thought to be associated satellite tumuli (陪塚), are also managed by the Imperial Household Agency.<ref name=":0" />

[[Category:Category]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tadeiyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Category]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tadeiyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[ja:田出井山古墳]] [[File:Tadeiyama Kofun zenkei-2.jpg|280px|thumb|center|Full view of the tumulus (front mound on the right, rear mound on the left){{Small|(View from Sakai City Hall's 21F observation lobby)}}]] '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' (田出井山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺区}}, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The actual burial individual is unknown, but it is designated as "'''Mozu Mimihara Kitano Misasagi'''" (百舌鳥耳原北陵) by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, attributed to {{ill|Emperor Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}. It is also part of the {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}} site, "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."
== Overview == Tadeiyama Kofun is located at the northern edge of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The selection of its location and the orientation of its tumulus are thought to have been determined with consideration of the view of Osaka Bay. The tumulus was constructed along the western edge of a plateau, with the main axis of the mound parallel to the contour lines and the front mound facing south.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000139|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus / Components / World Heritage / National Cultural Properties Database|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
The tumulus has a total length of approximately 148 meters. The rear mound has a diameter of about 76 meters and a height of about 13 meters, while the front mound has a width of about 110 meters and a height of approximately 15 meters. The mound is built in three tiers and features a protrusion only on the western side of the constriction area. It also has a shield-shaped inner moat.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/hanzeitenno.html|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun) - World Heritage Site Component Asset|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Sakai City Cultural and Tourism Bureau, Museum and Archaeology Division}}</ref>
Excavations revealed that the mound originally had a double moat structure. Cylindrical haniwa, haniwa in various shapes, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been unearthed from the site. Based on these artifacts, it is estimated that the tumulus was constructed in the mid-5th century.<ref name=":0" /> The Suzuyama Kofun and Tenno Kofun, thought to be associated satellite tumuli (陪塚), are also managed by the Imperial Household Agency.<ref name=":0" />
Historical maps and documents from the Edo period describe the site as "Hanzei Tennō-ryō" or "Tatei-no-misasagi." "Tatei" was a regional name that later evolved into "Tadei." The inner moat at the northeastern corner of the front mound is adjacent to the grounds of the nearby {{ill|Katanokami Shrine|ja|方違神社}}.
There is debate about the attribution of this tumulus as Emperor Hanzei's mausoleum due to its significantly smaller size compared to the other two tumuli identified as part of the Mozu Mimihara Sanryō (三陵): {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}} and {{ill|Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has also designated the larger {{ill|Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳}} as an auxiliary burial site for Emperor Hanzei.
<gallery> File:Suzuyama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|Suzuyama Kofun File:天王古墳.jpg|Tenno Kofun </gallery>
== Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts unearthed from the site include magatama made of agate, Sue ware vessels, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa, lid-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and parts of human-shaped haniwa. The haniwa fragments suggest a relatively early phase of the 5th century. Numerous cylindrical haniwa fragments have been found on the northeastern side of the rear mound.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sakai Cultural Heritage Research Overview, Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|year=1990}}</ref>
== Scale == The dimensions of the tumuli are as follows:<ref>{{Cite book|author="The Kofun Group" Production Committee|title=The Kofun Group: Mozu and Furuichi - All 89 Mounds|publisher=Nakashima Atsushi|year=2018|page=103}}</ref> * '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' ** Total length: 148 meters ** '''Rear mound''' (three tiers): ***** Diameter: 76 meters ***** Height: 13 meters ** '''Front mound''' (three tiers): ***** Width: 110 meters ***** Height: 14.8 meters * '''Suzuyama Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 22 meters ** Height: 3 meters * '''Tenno Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 11 meters ** Height: 3 meters
== World Heritage == In July 2019, Tadeiyama Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Heritage / Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020|title=World Heritage / Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
== Access == * '''Train''': A 5-minute walk east from {{ill|Sakai Higashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the Nankai Koya Line.
== References == {{Reflist}}

There is debate about the attribution of this tumulus as Emperor Hanzei's mausoleum due to its significantly smaller size compared to the other two tumuli identified as part of the Mozu Mimihara Sanryō (三陵): {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}} and {{ill|Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}}. <gallery> File:Suzuyama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|Suzuyama Kofun File:天王古墳.jpg|Tenno Kofun </gallery>
== Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts unearthed from the site include magatama made of agate, Sue ware vessels, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa, lid-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and parts of human-shaped haniwa. The haniwa fragments suggest a relatively early phase of the 5th century. Numerous cylindrical haniwa fragments have been found on the northeastern side of the rear mound.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sakai Cultural Heritage Research Overview, Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|year=1990}}</ref>
== Scale == The dimensions of the tumuli are as follows:<ref>{{Cite book|author="The Kofun Group" Production Committee|title=The Kofun Group: Mozu and Furuichi - All 89 Mounds|publisher=Nakashima Atsushi|year=2018|page=103}}</ref> * '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' ** Total length: 148 meters ** '''Rear mound''' (three tiers): ***** Diameter: 76 meters ***** Height: 13 meters ** '''Front mound''' (three tiers): ***** Width: 110 meters ***** Height: 14.8 meters * '''Suzuyama Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 22 meters ** Height: 3 meters * '''Tenno Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 11 meters ** Height: 3 meters
== World Heritage == In July 2019, Tadeiyama Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Heritage / Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020|title=World Heritage / Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
== Access == * '''Train''': A 5-minute walk east from {{ill|Sakai Higashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the Nankai Koya Line.
== References == {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tadeiyama Kofun}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tadeiyama Kofun}}[[ja:田出井山古墳]] [[File:Tadeiyama Kofun zenkei-2.jpg|280px|thumb|center|Full view of the tumulus (front mound on the right, rear mound on the left){{Small|(View from Sakai City Hall's 21F observation lobby)}}]] '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' (田出井山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺区}}, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The actual burial individual is unknown, but it is designated as "'''Mozu Mimihara Kitano Misasagi'''" (百舌鳥耳原北陵) by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, attributed to {{ill|Emperor Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}. It is also part of the {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}} site, "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."
== Overview == Tadeiyama Kofun is located at the northern edge of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The selection of its location and the orientation of its tumulus are thought to have been determined with consideration of the view of Osaka Bay. The tumulus was constructed along the western edge of a plateau, with the main axis of the mound parallel to the contour lines and the front mound facing south.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000139|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus / Components / World Heritage / National Cultural Properties Database|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
The tumulus has a total length of approximately 148 meters. The rear mound has a diameter of about 76 meters and a height of about 13 meters, while the front mound has a width of about 110 meters and a height of approximately 15 meters. The mound is built in three tiers and features a protrusion only on the western side of the constriction area. It also has a shield-shaped inner moat.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/hanzeitenno.html|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun) - World Heritage Site Component Asset|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Sakai City Cultural and Tourism Bureau, Museum and Archaeology Division}}</ref>
Excavations revealed that the mound originally had a double moat structure. Cylindrical haniwa, haniwa in various shapes, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been unearthed from the site. Based on these artifacts, it is estimated that the tumulus was constructed in the mid-5th century.<ref name=":0" /> The Suzuyama Kofun and Tenno Kofun, thought to be associated satellite tumuli (陪塚), are also managed by the Imperial Household Agency.<ref name=":0" />
Historical maps and documents from the Edo period describe the site as "Hanzei Tennō-ryō" or "Tatei-no-misasagi." "Tatei" was a regional name that later evolved into "Tadei." The inner moat at the northeastern corner of the front mound is adjacent to the grounds of the nearby {{ill|Katanokami Shrine|ja|方違神社}}.
There is debate about the attribution of this tumulus as Emperor Hanzei's mausoleum due to its significantly smaller size compared to the other two tumuli identified as part of the Mozu Mimihara Sanryō (三陵): {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}} and {{ill|Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has also designated the larger {{ill|Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳}} as an auxiliary burial site for Emperor Hanzei.
<gallery> File:Suzuyama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|Suzuyama Kofun File:天王古墳.jpg|Tenno Kofun </gallery>
== Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts unearthed from the site include magatama made of agate, Sue ware vessels, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa, lid-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and parts of human-shaped haniwa. The haniwa fragments suggest a relatively early phase of the 5th century. Numerous cylindrical haniwa fragments have been found on the northeastern side of the rear mound.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sakai Cultural Heritage Research Overview, Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|year=1990}}</ref>
== Scale == The dimensions of the tumuli are as follows:<ref>{{Cite book|author="The Kofun Group" Production Committee|title=The Kofun Group: Mozu and Furuichi - All 89 Mounds|publisher=Nakashima Atsushi|year=2018|page=103}}</ref> * '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' ** Total length: 148 meters ** '''Rear mound''' (three tiers): ***** Diameter: 76 meters ***** Height: 13 meters ** '''Front mound''' (three tiers): ***** Width: 110 meters ***** Height: 14.8 meters * '''Suzuyama Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 22 meters ** Height: 3 meters * '''Tenno Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 11 meters ** Height: 3 meters
== World Heritage == In July 2019, Tadeiyama Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Heritage / Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020|title=World Heritage / Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
== Access == * '''Train''': A 5-minute walk east from {{ill|Sakai Higashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the Nankai Koya Line.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tadeiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/018/index.html Mozu Mimihara Kitano Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/hanzeitenno.html Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun)] - Sakai City Website * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/133 Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun)] - Sakai Tourism and Convention Bureau * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/index.html World Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group] - Sakai City Office

[[Category:kk]][[Category:Mozu Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Osaka Prefecture's historical sites]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sakai-ku, Sakai]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Emperor Hanzei]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tadeiyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tadeiyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:kk]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] [[ja:田出井山古墳]]

[[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tadeiyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tadeiyama Kofun}}[[ja:田出井山古墳]] [[File:Tadeiyama Kofun zenkei-2.jpg|280px|thumb|center|Full view of the tumulus (front mound on the right, rear mound on the left){{Small|(View from Sakai City Hall's 21F observation lobby)}}]] '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' (田出井山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺区}}, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The actual burial individual is unknown, but it is designated as "'''Mozu Mimihara Kitano Misasagi'''" (百舌鳥耳原北陵) by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, attributed to {{ill|Emperor Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}. It is also part of the {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}} site, "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."
== Overview == Tadeiyama Kofun is located at the northern edge of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The selection of its location and the orientation of its tumulus are thought to have been determined with consideration of the view of Osaka Bay. The tumulus was constructed along the western edge of a plateau, with the main axis of the mound parallel to the contour lines and the front mound facing south.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000139|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus / Components / World Heritage / National Cultural Properties Database|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
The tumulus has a total length of approximately 148 meters. The rear mound has a diameter of about 76 meters and a height of about 13 meters, while the front mound has a width of about 110 meters and a height of approximately 15 meters. The mound is built in three tiers and features a protrusion only on the western side of the constriction area. It also has a shield-shaped inner moat.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/hanzeitenno.html|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun) - World Heritage Site Component Asset|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Sakai City Cultural and Tourism Bureau, Museum and Archaeology Division}}</ref>
Excavations revealed that the mound originally had a double moat structure. Cylindrical haniwa, haniwa in various shapes, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been unearthed from the site. Based on these artifacts, it is estimated that the tumulus was constructed in the mid-5th century.<ref name=":0" /> The Suzuyama Kofun and Tenno Kofun, thought to be associated satellite tumuli (陪塚), are also managed by the Imperial Household Agency.<ref name=":0" />
Historical maps and documents from the Edo period describe the site as "Hanzei Tennō-ryō" or "Tatei-no-misasagi." "Tatei" was a regional name that later evolved into "Tadei." The inner moat at the northeastern corner of the front mound is adjacent to the grounds of the nearby {{ill|Katanokami Shrine|ja|方違神社}}.
There is debate about the attribution of this tumulus as Emperor Hanzei's mausoleum due to its significantly smaller size compared to the other two tumuli identified as part of the Mozu Mimihara Sanryō (三陵): {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}} and {{ill|Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has also designated the larger {{ill|Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳}} as an auxiliary burial site for Emperor Hanzei.
<gallery> File:Suzuyama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|Suzuyama Kofun File:天王古墳.jpg|Tenno Kofun </gallery>
== Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts unearthed from the site include magatama made of agate, Sue ware vessels, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa, lid-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and parts of human-shaped haniwa. The haniwa fragments suggest a relatively early phase of the 5th century. Numerous cylindrical haniwa fragments have been found on the northeastern side of the rear mound.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sakai Cultural Heritage Research Overview, Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|year=1990}}</ref>
== Scale == The dimensions of the tumuli are as follows:<ref>{{Cite book|author="The Kofun Group" Production Committee|title=The Kofun Group: Mozu and Furuichi - All 89 Mounds|publisher=Nakashima Atsushi|year=2018|page=103}}</ref> * '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' ** Total length: 148 meters ** '''Rear mound''' (three tiers): ***** Diameter: 76 meters ***** Height: 13 meters ** '''Front mound''' (three tiers): ***** Width: 110 meters ***** Height: 14.8 meters * '''Suzuyama Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 22 meters ** Height: 3 meters * '''Tenno Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 11 meters ** Height: 3 meters
== World Heritage == In July 2019, Tadeiyama Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Heritage / Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020|title=World Heritage / Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
== Access == * '''Train''': A 5-minute walk east from {{ill|Sakai Higashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the Nankai Koya Line.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tadeiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/018/index.html Mozu Mimihara Kitano Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/hanzeitenno.html Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun)] - Sakai City Website * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/133 Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun)] - Sakai Tourism and Convention Bureau * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/index.html World Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group] - Sakai City Office
