[[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tadeiyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tadeiyama Kofun}}[[ja:田出井山古墳]] [[File:Tadeiyama Kofun zenkei-2.jpg|280px|thumb|center|Full view of the tumulus (front mound on the right, rear mound on the left){{Small|(View from Sakai City Hall's 21F observation lobby)}}]] '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' (田出井山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺区}}, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The actual burial individual is unknown, but it is designated as "'''Mozu Mimihara Kitano Misasagi'''" (百舌鳥耳原北陵) by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, attributed to {{ill|Emperor Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}. It is also part of the {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}} site, "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."
== Overview == Tadeiyama Kofun is located at the northern edge of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The selection of its location and the orientation of its tumulus are thought to have been determined with consideration of the view of Osaka Bay. The tumulus was constructed along the western edge of a plateau, with the main axis of the mound parallel to the contour lines and the front mound facing south.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000139|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus / Components / World Heritage / National Cultural Properties Database|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
The tumulus has a total length of approximately 148 meters. The rear mound has a diameter of about 76 meters and a height of about 13 meters, while the front mound has a width of about 110 meters and a height of approximately 15 meters. The mound is built in three tiers and features a protrusion only on the western side of the constriction area. It also has a shield-shaped inner moat.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/hanzeitenno.html|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun) - World Heritage Site Component Asset|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Sakai City Cultural and Tourism Bureau, Museum and Archaeology Division}}</ref>
Excavations revealed that the mound originally had a double moat structure. Cylindrical haniwa, haniwa in various shapes, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been unearthed from the site. Based on these artifacts, it is estimated that the tumulus was constructed in the mid-5th century.<ref name=":0" /> The Suzuyama Kofun and Tenno Kofun, thought to be associated satellite tumuli (陪塚), are also managed by the Imperial Household Agency.<ref name=":0" />
Historical maps and documents from the Edo period describe the site as "Hanzei Tennō-ryō" or "Tatei-no-misasagi." "Tatei" was a regional name that later evolved into "Tadei." The inner moat at the northeastern corner of the front mound is adjacent to the grounds of the nearby {{ill|Katanokami Shrine|ja|方違神社}}.
There is debate about the attribution of this tumulus as Emperor Hanzei's mausoleum due to its significantly smaller size compared to the other two tumuli identified as part of the Mozu Mimihara Sanryō (三陵): {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}} and {{ill|Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has also designated the larger {{ill|Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳}} as an auxiliary burial site for Emperor Hanzei.
<gallery> File:Suzuyama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|Suzuyama Kofun File:天王古墳.jpg|Tenno Kofun </gallery>
== Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts unearthed from the site include magatama made of agate, Sue ware vessels, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa, lid-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and parts of human-shaped haniwa. The haniwa fragments suggest a relatively early phase of the 5th century. Numerous cylindrical haniwa fragments have been found on the northeastern side of the rear mound.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sakai Cultural Heritage Research Overview, Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|year=1990}}</ref>
== Scale == The dimensions of the tumuli are as follows:<ref>{{Cite book|author="The Kofun Group" Production Committee|title=The Kofun Group: Mozu and Furuichi - All 89 Mounds|publisher=Nakashima Atsushi|year=2018|page=103}}</ref> * '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' ** Total length: 148 meters ** '''Rear mound''' (three tiers): ***** Diameter: 76 meters ***** Height: 13 meters ** '''Front mound''' (three tiers): ***** Width: 110 meters ***** Height: 14.8 meters * '''Suzuyama Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 22 meters ** Height: 3 meters * '''Tenno Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 11 meters ** Height: 3 meters
== World Heritage == In July 2019, Tadeiyama Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Heritage / Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020|title=World Heritage / Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
== Access == * '''Train''': A 5-minute walk east from {{ill|Sakai Higashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the Nankai Koya Line.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tadeiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/018/index.html Mozu Mimihara Kitano Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/hanzeitenno.html Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun)] - Sakai City Website * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/133 Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun)] - Sakai Tourism and Convention Bureau * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/index.html World Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group] - Sakai City Office

It also has a shield-shaped inner moat.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/hanzeitenno.html|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun) - World Heritage Site Component Asset|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Sakai City Cultural and Tourism Bureau, Museum and Archaeology Division}}</ref>

Based on these artifacts, it is estimated that the tumulus was constructed in the mid-5th century.<ref name=":0" /> The Suzuyama Kofun and Tenno Kofun, thought to be associated satellite tumuli (陪塚), are also managed by the Imperial Household Agency.<ref name=":0" />
Historical maps and documents from the Edo period describe the site as "Hanzei Tennō-ryō" or "Tatei-no-misasagi." "Tatei" was a regional name that later evolved into "Tadei." The inner moat at the northeastern corner of the front mound is adjacent to the grounds of the nearby {{ill|Katanokami Shrine|ja|方違神社}}.
There is debate about the attribution of this tumulus as Emperor Hanzei's mausoleum due to its significantly smaller size compared to the other two tumuli identified as part of the Mozu Mimihara Sanryō (三陵): {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}} and {{ill|Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has also designated the larger {{ill|Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳}} as an auxiliary burial site for Emperor Hanzei.
<gallery> File:Suzuyama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|Suzuyama Kofun File:天王古墳.jpg|Tenno Kofun </gallery>
== Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts unearthed from the site include magatama made of agate, Sue ware vessels, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa, lid-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and parts of human-shaped haniwa. The haniwa fragments suggest a relatively early phase of the 5th century. Numerous cylindrical haniwa fragments have been found on the northeastern side of the rear mound.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sakai Cultural Heritage Research Overview, Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|year=1990}}</ref>
== Scale == The dimensions of the tumuli are as follows:<ref>{{Cite book|author="The Kofun Group" Production Committee|title=The Kofun Group: Mozu and Furuichi - All 89 Mounds|publisher=Nakashima Atsushi|year=2018|page=103}}</ref> * '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' ** Total length: 148 meters ** '''Rear mound''' (three tiers): ***** Diameter: 76 meters ***** Height: 13 meters ** '''Front mound''' (three tiers): ***** Width: 110 meters ***** Height: 14.8 meters * '''Suzuyama Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 22 meters ** Height: 3 meters * '''Tenno Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 11 meters ** Height: 3 meters
== World Heritage == In July 2019, Tadeiyama Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Heritage / Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020|title=World Heritage / Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
== Access == * '''Train''': A 5-minute walk east from {{ill|Sakai Higashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the Nankai Koya Line.

[[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tadeiyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tadeiyama Kofun}}[[ja:田出井山古墳]] [[File:Tadeiyama Kofun zenkei-2.jpg|280px|thumb|center|Full view of the tumulus (front mound on the right, rear mound on the left){{Small|(View from Sakai City Hall's 21F observation lobby)}}]] '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' (田出井山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺区}}, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The actual burial individual is unknown, but it is designated as "'''Mozu Mimihara Kitano Misasagi'''" (百舌鳥耳原北陵) by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, attributed to {{ill|Emperor Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}. It is also part of the {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}} site, "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."
== Overview == Tadeiyama Kofun is located at the northern edge of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The selection of its location and the orientation of its tumulus are thought to have been determined with consideration of the view of Osaka Bay. The tumulus was constructed along the western edge of a plateau, with the main axis of the mound parallel to the contour lines and the front mound facing south.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000139|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus / Components / World Heritage / National Cultural Properties Database|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
The tumulus has a total length of approximately 148 meters. The rear mound has a diameter of about 76 meters and a height of about 13 meters, while the front mound has a width of about 110 meters and a height of approximately 15 meters. The mound is built in three tiers and features a protrusion only on the western side of the constriction area. It also has a shield-shaped inner moat.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/hanzeitenno.html|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun) - World Heritage Site Component Asset|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Sakai City Cultural and Tourism Bureau, Museum and Archaeology Division}}</ref>
Excavations revealed that the mound originally had a double moat structure. Cylindrical haniwa, haniwa in various shapes, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been unearthed from the site. Based on these artifacts, it is estimated that the tumulus was constructed in the mid-5th century.<ref name=":0" /> The Suzuyama Kofun and Tenno Kofun, thought to be associated satellite tumuli (陪塚), are also managed by the Imperial Household Agency.<ref name=":0" />
Historical maps and documents from the Edo period describe the site as "Hanzei Tennō-ryō" or "Tatei-no-misasagi." "Tatei" was a regional name that later evolved into "Tadei." The inner moat at the northeastern corner of the front mound is adjacent to the grounds of the nearby {{ill|Katanokami Shrine|ja|方違神社}}.
There is debate about the attribution of this tumulus as Emperor Hanzei's mausoleum due to its significantly smaller size compared to the other two tumuli identified as part of the Mozu Mimihara Sanryō (三陵): {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}} and {{ill|Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has also designated the larger {{ill|Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳}} as an auxiliary burial site for Emperor Hanzei.
<gallery> File:Suzuyama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|Suzuyama Kofun File:天王古墳.jpg|Tenno Kofun </gallery>
== Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts unearthed from the site include magatama made of agate, Sue ware vessels, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa, lid-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and parts of human-shaped haniwa. The haniwa fragments suggest a relatively early phase of the 5th century. Numerous cylindrical haniwa fragments have been found on the northeastern side of the rear mound.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sakai Cultural Heritage Research Overview, Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|year=1990}}</ref>
== Scale == The dimensions of the tumuli are as follows:<ref>{{Cite book|author="The Kofun Group" Production Committee|title=The Kofun Group: Mozu and Furuichi - All 89 Mounds|publisher=Nakashima Atsushi|year=2018|page=103}}</ref> * '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' ** Total length: 148 meters ** '''Rear mound''' (three tiers): ***** Diameter: 76 meters ***** Height: 13 meters ** '''Front mound''' (three tiers): ***** Width: 110 meters ***** Height: 14.8 meters * '''Suzuyama Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 22 meters ** Height: 3 meters * '''Tenno Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 11 meters ** Height: 3 meters
== World Heritage == In July 2019, Tadeiyama Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Heritage / Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020|title=World Heritage / Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
== Access == * '''Train''': A 5-minute walk east from {{ill|Sakai Higashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the Nankai Koya Line.

== Scale == The dimensions of the tumuli are as follows:<ref>{{Cite book|author="The Kofun Group" Production Committee|title=The Kofun Group: Mozu and Furuichi - All 89 Mounds|publisher=Nakashima Atsushi|year=2018|page=103}}</ref> * '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' ** Total length: 148 meters ** '''Rear mound''' (three tiers): ***** Diameter: 76 meters ***** Height: 13 meters ** '''Front mound''' (three tiers): ***** Width: 110 meters ***** Height: 14.8 meters * '''Suzuyama Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 22 meters ** Height: 3 meters * '''Tenno Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 11 meters ** Height: 3 meters
== World Heritage == In July 2019, Tadeiyama Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Heritage / Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020|title=World Heritage / Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>

[[Category:kk]] [[ja:田出井山古墳]]

[[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Tadeiyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tadeiyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Tadeiyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tadeiyama Kofun}}[[ja:田出井山古墳]] [[File:Tadeiyama Kofun zenkei-2.jpg|280px|thumb|center|Full view of the tumulus (front mound on the right, rear mound on the left){{Small|(View from Sakai City Hall's 21F observation lobby)}}]] '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' (田出井山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺区}}, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The actual burial individual is unknown, but it is designated as "'''Mozu Mimihara Kitano Misasagi'''" (百舌鳥耳原北陵) by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, attributed to {{ill|Emperor Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}. It is also part of the {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}} site, "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."
== Overview == Tadeiyama Kofun is located at the northern edge of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The selection of its location and the orientation of its tumulus are thought to have been determined with consideration of the view of Osaka Bay. The tumulus was constructed along the western edge of a plateau, with the main axis of the mound parallel to the contour lines and the front mound facing south.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000139|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus / Components / World Heritage / National Cultural Properties Database|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
The tumulus has a total length of approximately 148 meters. The rear mound has a diameter of about 76 meters and a height of about 13 meters, while the front mound has a width of about 110 meters and a height of approximately 15 meters. The mound is built in three tiers and features a protrusion only on the western side of the constriction area. It also has a shield-shaped inner moat.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/hanzeitenno.html|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun) - World Heritage Site Component Asset|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Sakai City Cultural and Tourism Bureau, Museum and Archaeology Division}}</ref>
Excavations revealed that the mound originally had a double moat structure. Cylindrical haniwa, haniwa in various shapes, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been unearthed from the site. Based on these artifacts, it is estimated that the tumulus was constructed in the mid-5th century.<ref name=":0" /> The Suzuyama Kofun and Tenno Kofun, thought to be associated satellite tumuli (陪塚), are also managed by the Imperial Household Agency.<ref name=":0" />
Historical maps and documents from the Edo period describe the site as "Hanzei Tennō-ryō" or "Tatei-no-misasagi." "Tatei" was a regional name that later evolved into "Tadei." The inner moat at the northeastern corner of the front mound is adjacent to the grounds of the nearby {{ill|Katanokami Shrine|ja|方違神社}}.
There is debate about the attribution of this tumulus as Emperor Hanzei's mausoleum due to its significantly smaller size compared to the other two tumuli identified as part of the Mozu Mimihara Sanryō (三陵): {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}} and {{ill|Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has also designated the larger {{ill|Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳}} as an auxiliary burial site for Emperor Hanzei.
<gallery> File:Suzuyama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|Suzuyama Kofun File:天王古墳.jpg|Tenno Kofun </gallery>
== Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts unearthed from the site include magatama made of agate, Sue ware vessels, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa, lid-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and parts of human-shaped haniwa. The haniwa fragments suggest a relatively early phase of the 5th century. Numerous cylindrical haniwa fragments have been found on the northeastern side of the rear mound.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sakai Cultural Heritage Research Overview, Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|year=1990}}</ref>
== Scale == The dimensions of the tumuli are as follows:<ref>{{Cite book|author="The Kofun Group" Production Committee|title=The Kofun Group: Mozu and Furuichi - All 89 Mounds|publisher=Nakashima Atsushi|year=2018|page=103}}</ref> * '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' ** Total length: 148 meters ** '''Rear mound''' (three tiers): ***** Diameter: 76 meters ***** Height: 13 meters ** '''Front mound''' (three tiers): ***** Width: 110 meters ***** Height: 14.8 meters * '''Suzuyama Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 22 meters ** Height: 3 meters * '''Tenno Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 11 meters ** Height: 3 meters
== World Heritage == In July 2019, Tadeiyama Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Heritage / Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020|title=World Heritage / Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
== Access == * '''Train''': A 5-minute walk east from {{ill|Sakai Higashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the Nankai Koya Line.

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:田出井山古墳]]

[[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tadeiyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tadeiyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tadeiyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tadeiyama Kofun}}[[ja:田出井山古墳]] [[File:Tadeiyama Kofun zenkei-2.jpg|280px|thumb|center|Full view of the tumulus (front mound on the right, rear mound on the left){{Small|(View from Sakai City Hall's 21F observation lobby)}}]] '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' (田出井山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺区}}, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The actual burial individual is unknown, but it is designated as "'''Mozu Mimihara Kitano Misasagi'''" (百舌鳥耳原北陵) by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, attributed to {{ill|Emperor Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}. It is also part of the {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}} site, "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."
== Overview == Tadeiyama Kofun is located at the northern edge of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The selection of its location and the orientation of its tumulus are thought to have been determined with consideration of the view of Osaka Bay. The tumulus was constructed along the western edge of a plateau, with the main axis of the mound parallel to the contour lines and the front mound facing south.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000139|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus / Components / World Heritage / National Cultural Properties Database|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
The tumulus has a total length of approximately 148 meters. The rear mound has a diameter of about 76 meters and a height of about 13 meters, while the front mound has a width of about 110 meters and a height of approximately 15 meters. The mound is built in three tiers and features a protrusion only on the western side of the constriction area. It also has a shield-shaped inner moat.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/hanzeitenno.html|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun) - World Heritage Site Component Asset|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Sakai City Cultural and Tourism Bureau, Museum and Archaeology Division}}</ref>
Excavations revealed that the mound originally had a double moat structure. Cylindrical haniwa, haniwa in various shapes, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been unearthed from the site. Based on these artifacts, it is estimated that the tumulus was constructed in the mid-5th century.<ref name=":0" /> The Suzuyama Kofun and Tenno Kofun, thought to be associated satellite tumuli (陪塚), are also managed by the Imperial Household Agency.<ref name=":0" />
Historical maps and documents from the Edo period describe the site as "Hanzei Tennō-ryō" or "Tatei-no-misasagi." "Tatei" was a regional name that later evolved into "Tadei." The inner moat at the northeastern corner of the front mound is adjacent to the grounds of the nearby {{ill|Katanokami Shrine|ja|方違神社}}.
There is debate about the attribution of this tumulus as Emperor Hanzei's mausoleum due to its significantly smaller size compared to the other two tumuli identified as part of the Mozu Mimihara Sanryō (三陵): {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}} and {{ill|Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has also designated the larger {{ill|Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳}} as an auxiliary burial site for Emperor Hanzei.
<gallery> File:Suzuyama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|Suzuyama Kofun File:天王古墳.jpg|Tenno Kofun </gallery>
== Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts unearthed from the site include magatama made of agate, Sue ware vessels, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa, lid-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and parts of human-shaped haniwa. The haniwa fragments suggest a relatively early phase of the 5th century. Numerous cylindrical haniwa fragments have been found on the northeastern side of the rear mound.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sakai Cultural Heritage Research Overview, Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|year=1990}}</ref>
== Scale == The dimensions of the tumuli are as follows:<ref>{{Cite book|author="The Kofun Group" Production Committee|title=The Kofun Group: Mozu and Furuichi - All 89 Mounds|publisher=Nakashima Atsushi|year=2018|page=103}}</ref> * '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' ** Total length: 148 meters ** '''Rear mound''' (three tiers): ***** Diameter: 76 meters ***** Height: 13 meters ** '''Front mound''' (three tiers): ***** Width: 110 meters ***** Height: 14.8 meters * '''Suzuyama Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 22 meters ** Height: 3 meters * '''Tenno Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 11 meters ** Height: 3 meters
== World Heritage == In July 2019, Tadeiyama Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Heritage / Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020|title=World Heritage / Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
== Access == * '''Train''': A 5-minute walk east from {{ill|Sakai Higashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the Nankai Koya Line.

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:田出井山古墳]]

[[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tadeiyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tadeiyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tadeiyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tadeiyama Kofun}}[[ja:田出井山古墳]] [[File:Tadeiyama Kofun zenkei-2.jpg|280px|thumb|center|Full view of the tumulus (front mound on the right, rear mound on the left){{Small|(View from Sakai City Hall's 21F observation lobby)}}]] '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' (田出井山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺区}}, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The actual burial individual is unknown, but it is designated as "'''Mozu Mimihara Kitano Misasagi'''" (百舌鳥耳原北陵) by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, attributed to {{ill|Emperor Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}. It is also part of the {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}} site, "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."
== Overview == Tadeiyama Kofun is located at the northern edge of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The selection of its location and the orientation of its tumulus are thought to have been determined with consideration of the view of Osaka Bay. The tumulus was constructed along the western edge of a plateau, with the main axis of the mound parallel to the contour lines and the front mound facing south.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000139|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus / Components / World Heritage / National Cultural Properties Database|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
The tumulus has a total length of approximately 148 meters. The rear mound has a diameter of about 76 meters and a height of about 13 meters, while the front mound has a width of about 110 meters and a height of approximately 15 meters. The mound is built in three tiers and features a protrusion only on the western side of the constriction area. It also has a shield-shaped inner moat.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/hanzeitenno.html|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun) - World Heritage Site Component Asset|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Sakai City Cultural and Tourism Bureau, Museum and Archaeology Division}}</ref>
Excavations revealed that the mound originally had a double moat structure. Cylindrical haniwa, haniwa in various shapes, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been unearthed from the site. Based on these artifacts, it is estimated that the tumulus was constructed in the mid-5th century.<ref name=":0" /> The Suzuyama Kofun and Tenno Kofun, thought to be associated satellite tumuli (陪塚), are also managed by the Imperial Household Agency.<ref name=":0" />
Historical maps and documents from the Edo period describe the site as "Hanzei Tennō-ryō" or "Tatei-no-misasagi." "Tatei" was a regional name that later evolved into "Tadei." The inner moat at the northeastern corner of the front mound is adjacent to the grounds of the nearby {{ill|Katanokami Shrine|ja|方違神社}}.
There is debate about the attribution of this tumulus as Emperor Hanzei's mausoleum due to its significantly smaller size compared to the other two tumuli identified as part of the Mozu Mimihara Sanryō (三陵): {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}} and {{ill|Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has also designated the larger {{ill|Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳}} as an auxiliary burial site for Emperor Hanzei.
<gallery> File:Suzuyama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|Suzuyama Kofun File:天王古墳.jpg|Tenno Kofun </gallery>
== Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts unearthed from the site include magatama made of agate, Sue ware vessels, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa, lid-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and parts of human-shaped haniwa. The haniwa fragments suggest a relatively early phase of the 5th century. Numerous cylindrical haniwa fragments have been found on the northeastern side of the rear mound.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sakai Cultural Heritage Research Overview, Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|year=1990}}</ref>
== Scale == The dimensions of the tumuli are as follows:<ref>{{Cite book|author="The Kofun Group" Production Committee|title=The Kofun Group: Mozu and Furuichi - All 89 Mounds|publisher=Nakashima Atsushi|year=2018|page=103}}</ref> * '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' ** Total length: 148 meters ** '''Rear mound''' (three tiers): ***** Diameter: 76 meters ***** Height: 13 meters ** '''Front mound''' (three tiers): ***** Width: 110 meters ***** Height: 14.8 meters * '''Suzuyama Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 22 meters ** Height: 3 meters * '''Tenno Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 11 meters ** Height: 3 meters
== World Heritage == In July 2019, Tadeiyama Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Heritage / Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020|title=World Heritage / Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
== Access == * '''Train''': A 5-minute walk east from {{ill|Sakai Higashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the Nankai Koya Line.

[[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tadeiyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tadeiyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tadeiyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tadeiyama Kofun}}[[ja:田出井山古墳]] [[File:Tadeiyama Kofun zenkei-2.jpg|280px|thumb|center|Full view of the tumulus (front mound on the right, rear mound on the left){{Small|(View from Sakai City Hall's 21F observation lobby)}}]] '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' (田出井山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺区}}, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The actual burial individual is unknown, but it is designated as "'''Mozu Mimihara Kitano Misasagi'''" (百舌鳥耳原北陵) by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, attributed to {{ill|Emperor Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}. It is also part of the {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}} site, "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."
== Overview == Tadeiyama Kofun is located at the northern edge of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The selection of its location and the orientation of its tumulus are thought to have been determined with consideration of the view of Osaka Bay. The tumulus was constructed along the western edge of a plateau, with the main axis of the mound parallel to the contour lines and the front mound facing south.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000139|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus / Components / World Heritage / National Cultural Properties Database|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
The tumulus has a total length of approximately 148 meters. The rear mound has a diameter of about 76 meters and a height of about 13 meters, while the front mound has a width of about 110 meters and a height of approximately 15 meters. The mound is built in three tiers and features a protrusion only on the western side of the constriction area. It also has a shield-shaped inner moat.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/hanzeitenno.html|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun) - World Heritage Site Component Asset|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Sakai City Cultural and Tourism Bureau, Museum and Archaeology Division}}</ref>
Excavations revealed that the mound originally had a double moat structure. Cylindrical haniwa, haniwa in various shapes, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been unearthed from the site. Based on these artifacts, it is estimated that the tumulus was constructed in the mid-5th century.<ref name=":0" /> The Suzuyama Kofun and Tenno Kofun, thought to be associated satellite tumuli (陪塚), are also managed by the Imperial Household Agency.<ref name=":0" />
Historical maps and documents from the Edo period describe the site as "Hanzei Tennō-ryō" or "Tatei-no-misasagi." "Tatei" was a regional name that later evolved into "Tadei." The inner moat at the northeastern corner of the front mound is adjacent to the grounds of the nearby {{ill|Katanokami Shrine|ja|方違神社}}.
There is debate about the attribution of this tumulus as Emperor Hanzei's mausoleum due to its significantly smaller size compared to the other two tumuli identified as part of the Mozu Mimihara Sanryō (三陵): {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}} and {{ill|Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has also designated the larger {{ill|Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳}} as an auxiliary burial site for Emperor Hanzei.
<gallery> File:Suzuyama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|Suzuyama Kofun File:天王古墳.jpg|Tenno Kofun </gallery>
== Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts unearthed from the site include magatama made of agate, Sue ware vessels, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa, lid-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and parts of human-shaped haniwa. The haniwa fragments suggest a relatively early phase of the 5th century. Numerous cylindrical haniwa fragments have been found on the northeastern side of the rear mound.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sakai Cultural Heritage Research Overview, Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|year=1990}}</ref>
== Scale == The dimensions of the tumuli are as follows:<ref>{{Cite book|author="The Kofun Group" Production Committee|title=The Kofun Group: Mozu and Furuichi - All 89 Mounds|publisher=Nakashima Atsushi|year=2018|page=103}}</ref> * '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' ** Total length: 148 meters ** '''Rear mound''' (three tiers): ***** Diameter: 76 meters ***** Height: 13 meters ** '''Front mound''' (three tiers): ***** Width: 110 meters ***** Height: 14.8 meters * '''Suzuyama Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 22 meters ** Height: 3 meters * '''Tenno Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 11 meters ** Height: 3 meters
== World Heritage == In July 2019, Tadeiyama Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Heritage / Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020|title=World Heritage / Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
== Access == * '''Train''': A 5-minute walk east from {{ill|Sakai Higashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the Nankai Koya Line.

== World Heritage == In July 2019, Tadeiyama Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Heritage / Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020|title=World Heritage / Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
== Access == * '''Train''': A 5-minute walk east from {{ill|Sakai Higashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the Nankai Koya Line.

[[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tadeiyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tadeiyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tadeiyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tadeiyama Kofun}}[[ja:田出井山古墳]] [[File:Tadeiyama Kofun zenkei-2.jpg|280px|thumb|center|Full view of the tumulus (front mound on the right, rear mound on the left){{Small|(View from Sakai City Hall's 21F observation lobby)}}]] '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' (田出井山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺区}}, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The actual burial individual is unknown, but it is designated as "'''Mozu Mimihara Kitano Misasagi'''" (百舌鳥耳原北陵) by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, attributed to {{ill|Emperor Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}. It is also part of the {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}} site, "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."
== Overview == Tadeiyama Kofun is located at the northern edge of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The selection of its location and the orientation of its tumulus are thought to have been determined with consideration of the view of Osaka Bay. The tumulus was constructed along the western edge of a plateau, with the main axis of the mound parallel to the contour lines and the front mound facing south.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000139|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus / Components / World Heritage / National Cultural Properties Database|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
The tumulus has a total length of approximately 148 meters. The rear mound has a diameter of about 76 meters and a height of about 13 meters, while the front mound has a width of about 110 meters and a height of approximately 15 meters. The mound is built in three tiers and features a protrusion only on the western side of the constriction area. It also has a shield-shaped inner moat.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/hanzeitenno.html|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun) - World Heritage Site Component Asset|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Sakai City Cultural and Tourism Bureau, Museum and Archaeology Division}}</ref>
Excavations revealed that the mound originally had a double moat structure. Cylindrical haniwa, haniwa in various shapes, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been unearthed from the site. Based on these artifacts, it is estimated that the tumulus was constructed in the mid-5th century.<ref name=":0" /> The Suzuyama Kofun and Tenno Kofun, thought to be associated satellite tumuli (陪塚), are also managed by the Imperial Household Agency.<ref name=":0" />
Historical maps and documents from the Edo period describe the site as "Hanzei Tennō-ryō" or "Tatei-no-misasagi." "Tatei" was a regional name that later evolved into "Tadei." The inner moat at the northeastern corner of the front mound is adjacent to the grounds of the nearby {{ill|Katanokami Shrine|ja|方違神社}}.
There is debate about the attribution of this tumulus as Emperor Hanzei's mausoleum due to its significantly smaller size compared to the other two tumuli identified as part of the Mozu Mimihara Sanryō (三陵): {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}} and {{ill|Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has also designated the larger {{ill|Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳}} as an auxiliary burial site for Emperor Hanzei.
<gallery> File:Suzuyama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|Suzuyama Kofun File:天王古墳.jpg|Tenno Kofun </gallery>
== Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts unearthed from the site include magatama made of agate, Sue ware vessels, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa, lid-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and parts of human-shaped haniwa. The haniwa fragments suggest a relatively early phase of the 5th century. Numerous cylindrical haniwa fragments have been found on the northeastern side of the rear mound.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sakai Cultural Heritage Research Overview, Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|year=1990}}</ref>
== Scale == The dimensions of the tumuli are as follows:<ref>{{Cite book|author="The Kofun Group" Production Committee|title=The Kofun Group: Mozu and Furuichi - All 89 Mounds|publisher=Nakashima Atsushi|year=2018|page=103}}</ref> * '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' ** Total length: 148 meters ** '''Rear mound''' (three tiers): ***** Diameter: 76 meters ***** Height: 13 meters ** '''Front mound''' (three tiers): ***** Width: 110 meters ***** Height: 14.8 meters * '''Suzuyama Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 22 meters ** Height: 3 meters * '''Tenno Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 11 meters ** Height: 3 meters
== World Heritage == In July 2019, Tadeiyama Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Heritage / Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020|title=World Heritage / Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
== Access == * '''Train''': A 5-minute walk east from {{ill|Sakai Higashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the Nankai Koya Line.

The inner moat at the northeastern corner of the front mound is adjacent to the grounds of the nearby {{ill|Katanokami Shrine|ja|方違神社}}.
There is debate about the attribution of this tumulus as Emperor Hanzei's mausoleum due to its significantly smaller size compared to the other two tumuli identified as part of the Mozu Mimihara Sanryō (三陵): {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}} and {{ill|Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has also designated the larger {{ill|Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳}} as an auxiliary burial site for Emperor Hanzei.
<gallery> File:Suzuyama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|Suzuyama Kofun File:天王古墳.jpg|Tenno Kofun </gallery>
== Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts unearthed from the site include magatama made of agate, Sue ware vessels, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa, lid-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and parts of human-shaped haniwa. The haniwa fragments suggest a relatively early phase of the 5th century. Numerous cylindrical haniwa fragments have been found on the northeastern side of the rear mound.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sakai Cultural Heritage Research Overview, Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|year=1990}}</ref>
== Scale == The dimensions of the tumuli are as follows:<ref>{{Cite book|author="The Kofun Group" Production Committee|title=The Kofun Group: Mozu and Furuichi - All 89 Mounds|publisher=Nakashima Atsushi|year=2018|page=103}}</ref> * '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' ** Total length: 148 meters ** '''Rear mound''' (three tiers): ***** Diameter: 76 meters ***** Height: 13 meters ** '''Front mound''' (three tiers): ***** Width: 110 meters ***** Height: 14.8 meters * '''Suzuyama Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 22 meters ** Height: 3 meters * '''Tenno Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 11 meters ** Height: 3 meters
== World Heritage == In July 2019, Tadeiyama Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Heritage / Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020|title=World Heritage / Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
== Access == * '''Train''': A 5-minute walk east from {{ill|Sakai Higashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the Nankai Koya Line.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tadeiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/018/index.html Mozu Mimihara Kitano Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/hanzeitenno.html Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun)] - Sakai City Website * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/133 Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun)] - Sakai Tourism and Convention Bureau * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/index.html World Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group] - Sakai City Office

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tadeiyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tadeiyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tadeiyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tadeiyama Kofun}}[[ja:田出井山古墳]] [[File:Tadeiyama Kofun zenkei-2.jpg|280px|thumb|center|Full view of the tumulus (front mound on the right, rear mound on the left){{Small|(View from Sakai City Hall's 21F observation lobby)}}]] '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' (田出井山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺区}}, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The actual burial individual is unknown, but it is designated as "'''Mozu Mimihara Kitano Misasagi'''" (百舌鳥耳原北陵) by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, attributed to {{ill|Emperor Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}. It is also part of the {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}} site, "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."
== Overview == Tadeiyama Kofun is located at the northern edge of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The selection of its location and the orientation of its tumulus are thought to have been determined with consideration of the view of Osaka Bay. The tumulus was constructed along the western edge of a plateau, with the main axis of the mound parallel to the contour lines and the front mound facing south.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000139|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus / Components / World Heritage / National Cultural Properties Database|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
The tumulus has a total length of approximately 148 meters. The rear mound has a diameter of about 76 meters and a height of about 13 meters, while the front mound has a width of about 110 meters and a height of approximately 15 meters. The mound is built in three tiers and features a protrusion only on the western side of the constriction area. It also has a shield-shaped inner moat.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/hanzeitenno.html|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun) - World Heritage Site Component Asset|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Sakai City Cultural and Tourism Bureau, Museum and Archaeology Division}}</ref>
Excavations revealed that the mound originally had a double moat structure. Cylindrical haniwa, haniwa in various shapes, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been unearthed from the site. Based on these artifacts, it is estimated that the tumulus was constructed in the mid-5th century.<ref name=":0" /> The Suzuyama Kofun and Tenno Kofun, thought to be associated satellite tumuli (陪塚), are also managed by the Imperial Household Agency.<ref name=":0" />
Historical maps and documents from the Edo period describe the site as "Hanzei Tennō-ryō" or "Tatei-no-misasagi." "Tatei" was a regional name that later evolved into "Tadei." The inner moat at the northeastern corner of the front mound is adjacent to the grounds of the nearby {{ill|Katanokami Shrine|ja|方違神社}}.
There is debate about the attribution of this tumulus as Emperor Hanzei's mausoleum due to its significantly smaller size compared to the other two tumuli identified as part of the Mozu Mimihara Sanryō (三陵): {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}} and {{ill|Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has also designated the larger {{ill|Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳}} as an auxiliary burial site for Emperor Hanzei.
<gallery> File:Suzuyama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|Suzuyama Kofun File:天王古墳.jpg|Tenno Kofun </gallery>
== Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts unearthed from the site include magatama made of agate, Sue ware vessels, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa, lid-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and parts of human-shaped haniwa. The haniwa fragments suggest a relatively early phase of the 5th century. Numerous cylindrical haniwa fragments have been found on the northeastern side of the rear mound.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sakai Cultural Heritage Research Overview, Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|year=1990}}</ref>
== Scale == The dimensions of the tumuli are as follows:<ref>{{Cite book|author="The Kofun Group" Production Committee|title=The Kofun Group: Mozu and Furuichi - All 89 Mounds|publisher=Nakashima Atsushi|year=2018|page=103}}</ref> * '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' ** Total length: 148 meters ** '''Rear mound''' (three tiers): ***** Diameter: 76 meters ***** Height: 13 meters ** '''Front mound''' (three tiers): ***** Width: 110 meters ***** Height: 14.8 meters * '''Suzuyama Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 22 meters ** Height: 3 meters * '''Tenno Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 11 meters ** Height: 3 meters
== World Heritage == In July 2019, Tadeiyama Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Heritage / Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020|title=World Heritage / Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
== Access == * '''Train''': A 5-minute walk east from {{ill|Sakai Higashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the Nankai Koya Line.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tadeiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/018/index.html Mozu Mimihara Kitano Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/hanzeitenno.html Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun)] - Sakai City Website * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/133 Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun)] - Sakai Tourism and Convention Bureau * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/index.html World Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group] - Sakai City Office

[[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tadeiyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tadeiyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tadeiyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tadeiyama Kofun}}[[ja:田出井山古墳]] [[File:Tadeiyama Kofun zenkei-2.jpg|280px|thumb|center|Full view of the tumulus (front mound on the right, rear mound on the left){{Small|(View from Sakai City Hall's 21F observation lobby)}}]] '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' (田出井山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺区}}, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The actual burial individual is unknown, but it is designated as "'''Mozu Mimihara Kitano Misasagi'''" (百舌鳥耳原北陵) by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, attributed to {{ill|Emperor Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}. It is also part of the {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}} site, "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."
== Overview == Tadeiyama Kofun is located at the northern edge of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The selection of its location and the orientation of its tumulus are thought to have been determined with consideration of the view of Osaka Bay. The tumulus was constructed along the western edge of a plateau, with the main axis of the mound parallel to the contour lines and the front mound facing south.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000139|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus / Components / World Heritage / National Cultural Properties Database|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
The tumulus has a total length of approximately 148 meters. The rear mound has a diameter of about 76 meters and a height of about 13 meters, while the front mound has a width of about 110 meters and a height of approximately 15 meters. The mound is built in three tiers and features a protrusion only on the western side of the constriction area. It also has a shield-shaped inner moat.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/hanzeitenno.html|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun) - World Heritage Site Component Asset|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Sakai City Cultural and Tourism Bureau, Museum and Archaeology Division}}</ref>
Excavations revealed that the mound originally had a double moat structure. Cylindrical haniwa, haniwa in various shapes, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been unearthed from the site. Based on these artifacts, it is estimated that the tumulus was constructed in the mid-5th century.<ref name=":0" /> The Suzuyama Kofun and Tenno Kofun, thought to be associated satellite tumuli (陪塚), are also managed by the Imperial Household Agency.<ref name=":0" />
Historical maps and documents from the Edo period describe the site as "Hanzei Tennō-ryō" or "Tatei-no-misasagi." "Tatei" was a regional name that later evolved into "Tadei." The inner moat at the northeastern corner of the front mound is adjacent to the grounds of the nearby {{ill|Katanokami Shrine|ja|方違神社}}.
There is debate about the attribution of this tumulus as Emperor Hanzei's mausoleum due to its significantly smaller size compared to the other two tumuli identified as part of the Mozu Mimihara Sanryō (三陵): {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}} and {{ill|Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has also designated the larger {{ill|Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳}} as an auxiliary burial site for Emperor Hanzei.
<gallery> File:Suzuyama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|Suzuyama Kofun File:天王古墳.jpg|Tenno Kofun </gallery>
== Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts unearthed from the site include magatama made of agate, Sue ware vessels, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa, lid-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and parts of human-shaped haniwa. The haniwa fragments suggest a relatively early phase of the 5th century. Numerous cylindrical haniwa fragments have been found on the northeastern side of the rear mound.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sakai Cultural Heritage Research Overview, Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|year=1990}}</ref>
== Scale == The dimensions of the tumuli are as follows:<ref>{{Cite book|author="The Kofun Group" Production Committee|title=The Kofun Group: Mozu and Furuichi - All 89 Mounds|publisher=Nakashima Atsushi|year=2018|page=103}}</ref> * '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' ** Total length: 148 meters ** '''Rear mound''' (three tiers): ***** Diameter: 76 meters ***** Height: 13 meters ** '''Front mound''' (three tiers): ***** Width: 110 meters ***** Height: 14.8 meters * '''Suzuyama Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 22 meters ** Height: 3 meters * '''Tenno Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 11 meters ** Height: 3 meters
== World Heritage == In July 2019, Tadeiyama Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Heritage / Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020|title=World Heritage / Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
== Access == * '''Train''': A 5-minute walk east from {{ill|Sakai Higashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the Nankai Koya Line.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tadeiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/018/index.html Mozu Mimihara Kitano Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/hanzeitenno.html Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun)] - Sakai City Website * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/133 Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun)] - Sakai Tourism and Convention Bureau * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/index.html World Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group] - Sakai City Office

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[ja:田出井山古墳]]

[[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tadeiyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tadeiyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tadeiyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tadeiyama Kofun}}[[ja:田出井山古墳]] [[File:Tadeiyama Kofun zenkei-2.jpg|280px|thumb|center|Full view of the tumulus (front mound on the right, rear mound on the left){{Small|(View from Sakai City Hall's 21F observation lobby)}}]] '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' (田出井山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺区}}, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The actual burial individual is unknown, but it is designated as "'''Mozu Mimihara Kitano Misasagi'''" (百舌鳥耳原北陵) by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, attributed to {{ill|Emperor Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}. It is also part of the {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}} site, "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."
== Overview == Tadeiyama Kofun is located at the northern edge of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The selection of its location and the orientation of its tumulus are thought to have been determined with consideration of the view of Osaka Bay. The tumulus was constructed along the western edge of a plateau, with the main axis of the mound parallel to the contour lines and the front mound facing south.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000139|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus / Components / World Heritage / National Cultural Properties Database|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
The tumulus has a total length of approximately 148 meters. The rear mound has a diameter of about 76 meters and a height of about 13 meters, while the front mound has a width of about 110 meters and a height of approximately 15 meters. The mound is built in three tiers and features a protrusion only on the western side of the constriction area. It also has a shield-shaped inner moat.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/hanzeitenno.html|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun) - World Heritage Site Component Asset|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Sakai City Cultural and Tourism Bureau, Museum and Archaeology Division}}</ref>
Excavations revealed that the mound originally had a double moat structure. Cylindrical haniwa, haniwa in various shapes, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been unearthed from the site. Based on these artifacts, it is estimated that the tumulus was constructed in the mid-5th century.<ref name=":0" /> The Suzuyama Kofun and Tenno Kofun, thought to be associated satellite tumuli (陪塚), are also managed by the Imperial Household Agency.<ref name=":0" />
Historical maps and documents from the Edo period describe the site as "Hanzei Tennō-ryō" or "Tatei-no-misasagi." "Tatei" was a regional name that later evolved into "Tadei." The inner moat at the northeastern corner of the front mound is adjacent to the grounds of the nearby {{ill|Katanokami Shrine|ja|方違神社}}.
There is debate about the attribution of this tumulus as Emperor Hanzei's mausoleum due to its significantly smaller size compared to the other two tumuli identified as part of the Mozu Mimihara Sanryō (三陵): {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳}} and {{ill|Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has also designated the larger {{ill|Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳}} as an auxiliary burial site for Emperor Hanzei.
<gallery> File:Suzuyama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|Suzuyama Kofun File:天王古墳.jpg|Tenno Kofun </gallery>
== Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts unearthed from the site include magatama made of agate, Sue ware vessels, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa, lid-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and parts of human-shaped haniwa. The haniwa fragments suggest a relatively early phase of the 5th century. Numerous cylindrical haniwa fragments have been found on the northeastern side of the rear mound.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sakai Cultural Heritage Research Overview, Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|year=1990}}</ref>
== Scale == The dimensions of the tumuli are as follows:<ref>{{Cite book|author="The Kofun Group" Production Committee|title=The Kofun Group: Mozu and Furuichi - All 89 Mounds|publisher=Nakashima Atsushi|year=2018|page=103}}</ref> * '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' ** Total length: 148 meters ** '''Rear mound''' (three tiers): ***** Diameter: 76 meters ***** Height: 13 meters ** '''Front mound''' (three tiers): ***** Width: 110 meters ***** Height: 14.8 meters * '''Suzuyama Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 22 meters ** Height: 3 meters * '''Tenno Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 11 meters ** Height: 3 meters
== World Heritage == In July 2019, Tadeiyama Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Heritage / Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020|title=World Heritage / Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
== Access == * '''Train''': A 5-minute walk east from {{ill|Sakai Higashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the Nankai Koya Line.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Tadeiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/018/index.html Mozu Mimihara Kitano Mausoleum] - Imperial Household Agency * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/hanzeitenno.html Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun)] - Sakai City Website * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/133 Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun)] - Sakai Tourism and Convention Bureau * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/index.html World Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group] - Sakai City Office

The mound is built in three tiers and features a protrusion only on the western side of the constriction area. Excavations revealed that the mound originally had a double moat structure. Cylindrical haniwa, haniwa in various shapes, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been unearthed from the site. Based on these artifacts, it is estimated that the tumulus was constructed in the mid-5th century.<ref name=":0" /> The Suzuyama Kofun and Tenno Kofun, thought to be associated satellite tumuli (陪塚), are also managed by the Imperial Household Agency.<ref name=":0" />
Historical maps and documents from the Edo period describe the site as "Hanzei Tennō-ryō" or "Tatei-no-misasagi." "Tatei" was a regional name that later evolved into "Tadei." The inner moat at the northeastern corner of the front mound is adjacent to the grounds of the nearby {{ill|Katanokami Shrine|ja|方違神社}}.
There is debate about the attribution of this tumulus as Emperor Hanzei's mausoleum due to its significantly smaller size compared to the other two tumuli identified as part of the Mozu Mimihara Sanryō (三陵): {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}} and {{ill|Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has also designated the larger {{ill|Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳}} as an auxiliary burial site for Emperor Hanzei.
<gallery> File:Suzuyama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|Suzuyama Kofun File:天王古墳.jpg|Tenno Kofun </gallery>
== Excavated Artifacts == Artifacts unearthed from the site include magatama made of agate, Sue ware vessels, and haniwa, including cylindrical haniwa, lid-shaped haniwa, house-shaped haniwa, and parts of human-shaped haniwa. The haniwa fragments suggest a relatively early phase of the 5th century. Numerous cylindrical haniwa fragments have been found on the northeastern side of the rear mound.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sakai Cultural Heritage Research Overview, Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|year=1990}}</ref>
== Scale == The dimensions of the tumuli are as follows:<ref>{{Cite book|author="The Kofun Group" Production Committee|title=The Kofun Group: Mozu and Furuichi - All 89 Mounds|publisher=Nakashima Atsushi|year=2018|page=103}}</ref> * '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' ** Total length: 148 meters ** '''Rear mound''' (three tiers): ***** Diameter: 76 meters ***** Height: 13 meters ** '''Front mound''' (three tiers): ***** Width: 110 meters ***** Height: 14.8 meters * '''Suzuyama Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 22 meters ** Height: 3 meters * '''Tenno Kofun (satellite mound)''': Square-shaped tumulus ** Total length: 11 meters ** Height: 3 meters
== World Heritage == In July 2019, Tadeiyama Kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage site "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=World Heritage / Cultural Heritage Online|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020|title=World Heritage / Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
== Access == * '''Train''': A 5-minute walk east from {{ill|Sakai Higashi Station|ja|堺東駅}} on the Nankai Koya Line.

[[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq-Tadeiyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tadeiyama Kofun}}[[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq-Tadeiyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tadeiyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tadeiyama Kofun}}[[ja:田出井山古墳]] [[File:Tadeiyama Kofun zenkei-2.jpg|280px|thumb|center|Full view of the tumulus (front mound on the right, rear mound on the left){{Small|(View from Sakai City Hall's 21F observation lobby)}}]] '''Tadeiyama Kofun''' (田出井山古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai, Osaka|ja|堺区}}, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The actual burial individual is unknown, but it is designated as "'''Mozu Mimihara Kitano Misasagi'''" (百舌鳥耳原北陵) by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}, attributed to {{ill|Emperor Hanzei|ja|反正天皇}}. It is also part of the {{ill|World Cultural Heritage|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}} site, "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."
== Overview == Tadeiyama Kofun is located at the northern edge of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Cluster|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. The selection of its location and the orientation of its tumulus are thought to have been determined with consideration of the view of Osaka Bay. The tumulus was constructed along the western edge of a plateau, with the main axis of the mound parallel to the contour lines and the front mound facing south.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000139|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus / Components / World Heritage / National Cultural Properties Database|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
The tumulus has a total length of approximately 148 meters. The rear mound has a diameter of about 76 meters and a height of about 13 meters, while the front mound has a width of about 110 meters and a height of approximately 15 meters. The mound is built in three tiers and features a protrusion only on the western side of the constriction area. It also has a shield-shaped inner moat.<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/hanzeitenno.html|title=Hanzei Tennō-ryō Tumulus (Tadeiyama Kofun) - World Heritage Site Component Asset|accessdate=2021-11-22|publisher=Sakai City Cultural and Tourism Bureau, Museum and Archaeology Division}}</ref>
Excavations revealed that the mound originally had a double moat structure. Cylindrical haniwa, haniwa in various shapes, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been unearthed from the site. Based on these artifacts, it is estimated that the tumulus was constructed in the mid-5th century.<ref name=":0" /> The Suzuyama Kofun and Tenno Kofun, thought to be associated satellite tumuli (陪塚), are also managed by the Imperial Household Agency.<ref name=":0" />
Historical maps and documents from the Edo period describe the site as "Hanzei Tennō-ryō" or "Tatei-no-misasagi." "Tatei" was a regional name that later evolved into "Tadei." The inner moat at the northeastern corner of the front mound is adjacent to the grounds of the nearby {{ill|Katanokami Shrine|ja|方違神社}}.
There is debate about the attribution of this tumulus as Emperor Hanzei's mausoleum due to its significantly smaller size compared to the other two tumuli identified as part of the Mozu Mimihara Sanryō (三陵): {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙陵古墳|1=Daisenryō Kofun}} and {{ill|Kamiishizu Misanzai Kofun|ja|上石津ミサンザイ古墳|1=Kami-Ishizu Misanzai Kofun}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has also designated the larger {{ill|Nisanzai Kofun|ja|土師ニサンザイ古墳}} as an auxiliary burial site for Emperor Hanzei.
<gallery> File:Suzuyama Kofun (Sakai) zenkei.JPG|Suzuyama Kofun File:天王古墳.jpg|Tenno Kofun </gallery>

Numerous cylindrical haniwa fragments have been found on the northeastern side of the rear mound.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Sakai Cultural Heritage Research Overview, Volume 1|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|year=1990}}</ref>
